docs.title: Memex Documentation
docs.text:
A self-hosted, distributed multi-agent AI system for home automation, coding, creative media, and voice interaction.

Version 3.4 · Phase 6 Complete · All inference runs on-premises  no external AI services.

docs.title: What is Memex?
docs.text:
Memex (also called Memex or Home AI Lab) is a production multi-agent system running across three physical nodes on a local network. It provides:

  • Intelligent chat and coding with inference-time quality verification (MarsRL)

  • Image, 3D, and action figure generation via ComfyUI pipelines

  • Voice interaction with BMO character synthesis and TTS

  • Smart home control through Home Assistant integration

  • Zero-trust security using SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity and JWT-ACE tokens


graph TB

    subgraph Gateway["Gateway Node · Turing<br/>192.168.2.103"]

        Traefik[Traefik Reverse Proxy]

        hollerith[hollerith Dashboards]

        jacquard[jacquard Metrics]

        knuth[knuth Logs]

    end



    subgraph Execution["Execution Node · Lovelace<br/>192.168.2.101"]

        Runtime[Agent Runtime · FastAPI]

        Ollama[Ollama · LLM Inference]

        ComfyUI[ComfyUI · Image Gen]

        Voice[Voice Engine · TTS]

    end



    subgraph Control["Control Node<br/>192.168.2.102"]

        SPIRE[SPIRE Server]

        Langfuse[Langfuse · Tracing]

        PostgreSQL[(PostgreSQL)]

        MemPalace[MemPalace · Memory]

    end



    Traefik --> Runtime

    Runtime --> Ollama

    Runtime --> ComfyUI

    Runtime --> Voice

    Runtime --> SPIRE

    Runtime --> Langfuse

    Runtime --> PostgreSQL

    Runtime --> MemPalace

docs.title: BMO Complete Guide
docs.text: BMO is an animated robot companion inspired by Adventure Time. It runs a full voice pipeline: wake-word detection on a Raspberry Pi → speech-to-text → LLM response (Ollama) → emotion-aware TTS + RVC voice conversion → animated face.

docs.title: Nodes
docs.text: Host IP Role R730 (shivelyserver) 192.168.2.103 GPU inference — Ollama, bmo-voice Docker Wyse 5070 (control) 192.168.2.102 Control plane Home Assistant 192.168.2.100 Smart home integration Raspberry Pi (BMO) DHCP Wake-word satellite, face display

docs.title: Services
docs.text: Service URL Purpose Ollama 192.168.2.103:11434 LLM inference bmo-voice 192.168.2.103:8100 TTS (/speak) + STT (/listen) agent-runtime 192.168.2.103:8008 Main agent orchestrator

docs.title: Complete Pipeline
docs.text:
User says "Hey Beemo"
        │
        ▼
[Raspberry Pi] voice_satellite.py
  └─ openwakeword (hey_beeMo.onnx) detects wake phrase
  └─ play_wake_ping()  →  chime (C6 → E6)
  └─ face: "acknowledged"
  └─ sd.rec() 3.8 s at 48 kHz → decimate 3× → 16 kHz
        │
        ▼  POST /listen (WAV)
[R730:8100] bmo-voice/server.py
  └─ faster-whisper medium.en → transcript text
        │
        ▼  POST /v1/voice/chat
[R730:8008] agent-runtime
  └─ Ollama LLM → BMO persona response text
        │
        ▼  detect_emotion() → pitch + speed
  Check voice_samples_map → pre-recorded clip match?
  ├─ YES → play_audio(sample_path)
  └─ NO  → POST /speak (text, pitch)
              └─ Kokoro TTS → RVC (bmo.pth / bmo.index) → WAV
        │
        ▼
[Raspberry Pi] aplay via HDMI
  └─ extract_mouth_sync_from_wav() → RMS envelope
  └─ pygame_face: emotion expression + mouth animation
  └─ reset to neutral

docs.title: Sandbox – Quick Testing Without Hardware
docs.text: scripts/bmo_sandbox.py exercises the full pipeline from a Windows terminal. No Raspberry Pi required.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11: C:\Users\panca\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\python.exe
  • requests package: pip install requests --user
  • R730 reachable at 192.168.2.103

docs.title: Preflight Check
docs.text:
Always run preflight first to verify all services are up:

& "C:\Users\panca\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\python.exe" `
  scripts/bmo_sandbox.py `
  --host 192.168.2.103 --port 11434 --model llama3.2:3b `
  --preflight

Expected output:

  Ollama API          ✅ OK     http://192.168.2.103:11434
  Model llama3.2:3b   ✅ OK     available
  BMO Voice (TTS)     ✅ OK     http://192.168.2.103:8100
  Agent imports       ✅ OK     ok
  All critical services OK — sandbox is ready.

BMO Voice shows ⚠️ WARN

The bmo_voice_gpu container is not running. See bmo-voice Service below.

docs.title: Single Prompt
docs.text:
& "C:\Users\panca\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\python.exe" `
  scripts/bmo_sandbox.py `
  --host 192.168.2.103 --port 11434 --model llama3.2:3b `
  --prompt "Hey BMO, who wants to play video games?"

Add --tts to also generate and save the audio response (saved to delivered_artifacts/):

& "C:\Users\panca\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\python.exe" `
  scripts/bmo_sandbox.py `
  --host 192.168.2.103 --port 11434 --model llama3.2:3b `
  --tts --prompt "Hey BMO, say hello"

docs.title: Interactive Mode (REPL)
docs.text:
& "C:\Users\panca\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\python.exe" `
  scripts/bmo_sandbox.py `
  --host 192.168.2.103 --port 11434 --model llama3.2:3b

REPL commands:

Command Effect (type anything) Full pipeline: sample match → LLM → emotion → optional TTS /emotion sad Force emotion for next response /sample hello Play a pre-recorded voice sample by key /prompt Multi-line prompt entry /quit Exit

docs.title: Batch Mode
docs.text:
& "C:\Users\panca\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python311\python.exe" `
  scripts/bmo_sandbox.py `
  --host 192.168.2.103 --port 11434 --model llama3.2:3b `
  --batch tests/bmo_test_prompts.txt

docs.title: CLI Flags Reference
docs.text: Flag Default Description --host 192.168.2.103 Ollama host --port 11434 Ollama port --model $BMO_LLM_MODEL or qwen3:14b LLM model name --tts off Enable TTS audio generation --tts-host same as --host bmo-voice host --tts-port 8100 bmo-voice port --prompt TEXT — Single-shot prompt --batch FILE — Batch test file --preflight — Check services and exit

docs.title: Response Quality Warnings
docs.text:
The sandbox automatically warns about BMO persona violations:

Warning Rule Markdown / emojis detected BMO never uses markdown or emoji First-person pronoun Should say "Beemo will…" not "I will…" AI self-reference Must never say "As an AI" or "language model" Numeric digits Must spell out: "twenty three" not "23" "BMO" instead of "Beemo" TTS engine pronounces "Beemo" correctly Response > 300 chars Too long for voice delivery

docs.title: Voice Operation – Raspberry Pi Satellite
docs.text: scripts/voice_satellite.py runs on the Raspberry Pi.

docs.title: Key Configuration
docs.text:
Edit these constants at the top of voice_satellite.py:

WAKE_WORD_MODELS = ["hey_beeMo"]    # Custom ONNX model name (no extension)
THRESHOLD        = 0.5              # Wake-word confidence (0–1)
SAMPLE_RATE      = 16000            # Target rate for openwakeword
CHUNK_SIZE       = 1280             # 80 ms chunks at 16 kHz
POST_INTERACTION_COOLDOWN = 2.0     # Seconds to ignore BMO's own audio

HOST_IP          = os.getenv("LOVELACE_IP", "192.168.2.103")
VOICE_ENGINE_URL = f"http://{HOST_IP}:8020/stt"
AGENT_URL        = f"http://{HOST_IP}:8000/v1/voice/chat"

docs.title: Raspberry Pi Setup
docs.text:
# System packages
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y \
  python3-pip python3-venv portaudio19-dev libsndfile1 espeak-ng

# Project directory and venv
mkdir -p ~/bmo_client && cd ~/bmo_client
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate

pip install sounddevice numpy requests openwakeword

# Copy files from dev machine
scp scripts/voice_satellite.py misterobots@<PI_IP>:~/bmo_client/
scp agents/bmo_voice/hey_beeMo.onnx misterobots@<PI_IP>:~/bmo_client/

docs.title: Running the Satellite
docs.text:
cd ~/bmo_client
source venv/bin/activate
python voice_satellite.py

BMO prints heartbeat logs every 5 seconds while waiting. Say "Hey Beemo" to trigger the interaction.

docs.title: Wake-Word Model Resolution
docs.text:
The satellite searches for hey_beeMo.onnx in this order:

  1. Current working directory
  2. The script's own directory
  3. Repository root
  4. agents/bmo_voice/ relative to repo root

Fallback model

If hey_beeMo.onnx is not found, the satellite silently falls back to hey_jarvis_v0.1. Check satellite logs for the loaded model name and ensure hey_beeMo.onnx is co-located with voice_satellite.py.

docs.title: Running as a Systemd Service
docs.text:
sudo cp agents/bmo_voice/bmo_satellite.service /etc/systemd/system/
sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/bmo_satellite.service   # set LOVELACE_IP and paths

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable bmo_satellite
sudo systemctl start bmo_satellite

# Check
sudo systemctl status bmo_satellite
journalctl -u bmo_satellite -f

docs.title: Full BMO Experience (Face + Voice)
docs.text:
Run bmo_driver.py for the animated face + voice pipeline:

python bmo_driver.py \
  --host 192.168.2.103 \
  --port 8100 \
  --pitch 3 \
  --output_device 1 \
  --volume 80
Flag Default Description --host required R730 IP --port 8100 bmo-voice port --input_device auto ALSA mic device index --output_device auto ALSA speaker (name or index) --volume 80 Speaker volume 0–100 --pitch 3 TTS pitch offset (semitones) --method rmvpe RVC pitch algorithm: rmvpe, crepe, harvest

Headless Pi display

Set SDL_VIDEODRIVER=kmsdrm for native HDMI framebuffer (no X11 needed).

docs.title: HDMI Audio Setup
docs.text:
aplay -l                               # Find the HDMI card number N
aplay -D plughw:N,0 <test.wav>         # Verify playback

# Soft volume (optional) — edit slave.pcm to match card number
cp agents/bmo_voice/asoundrc.example ~/.asoundrc

docs.title: Container Details
docs.text: Property Value Container name bmo_voice_gpu Port mapping 8100 → 8000 GPU NVIDIA RTX 3070 Ti (all devices) Model files agents/bmo_voice/models/

docs.title: Start / Stop
docs.text:
ssh misterobots@192.168.2.103
cd ~/Home_AI_Lab/execution_plane

docker compose up -d bmo-voice          # Start
docker compose stop bmo-voice           # Stop
docker compose restart bmo-voice        # Restart
docker ps --filter name=bmo_voice_gpu   # Status

docs.title: Rebuild After Code Changes
docs.text:
cd ~/Home_AI_Lab/execution_plane
docker compose build bmo-voice
docker compose up -d bmo-voice

First build time

The first build takes ~25 minutes (large CUDA image). Subsequent code-only rebuilds take ~2 minutes (pip cache layer is preserved).

docs.title: Container Logs
docs.text:
docker logs bmo_voice_gpu --tail=50
docker logs bmo_voice_gpu -f

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
POST /speakPOST /listenGET /health

Generate audio in BMO's voice.

curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103:8100/speak \
  -F "text=Beemo wants to play video games!" \
  -F "pitch=3" \
  -F "speed=1.15" \
  --output bmo_response.wav
Parameter Default Description text required Text to synthesize pitch 0 Semitone shift (positive = higher) speed 1.0 Speed multiplier method rmvpe RVC pitch: rmvpe, crepe, harvest

Response: audio/wav binary.

Transcribe audio using Whisper.

curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103:8100/listen \
  -F "file=@recording.wav"

Response: 

{"text": "hey beemo who wants to play video games"}


curl http://192.168.2.103:8100/health

docs.title: Required Model Files
docs.text:
The container expects these files in agents/bmo_voice/models/:

File Purpose models/bmo.pth RVC voice model weights models/bmo.index RVC feature index

Missing models

If these files are absent, /speak returns HTTP 500. /listen and /health still work without them.

docs.title: Pre-Recorded Sample Fast-Path
docs.text:
These phrases bypass the LLM and play a pre-recorded clip directly:

Phrase Sample file hello Intro02_Hello_ItsMeBEEMO.wav who wants to play video games General_Games01_whoWantsToPlayVideoGames.wav victory Player_Win07_VICTORY.wav game over Player_Lose09_GAMEOVER.wav hahaha Giggle01.wav

docs.title: Direct Ollama Test
docs.text:
curl -s http://192.168.2.103:11434/api/generate \
  -d '{"model":"llama3.2:3b","prompt":"Say hello as BMO","stream":false}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['response'])"

docs.title: Direct bmo-voice Test
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103:8100/speak \
  -F "text=Beemo says hello!" -F "pitch=3" \
  --output /tmp/bmo_test.wav
aplay /tmp/bmo_test.wav

docs.title: Monitor BMO State (UDP)
docs.text:
python scripts/bmo_monitor.py
# Listens on UDP port 8123, prints colour-coded state events

docs.title: Sandbox: Ollama Timeout
docs.text:
Symptom: --prompt hangs for 30 s then errors.

The sandbox automatically falls back from /api/chat to /api/generate. If both fail, verify the model name:

curl http://192.168.2.103:11434/api/tags

docs.title: Sandbox: TTS ⚠️ WARN in Preflight
docs.text:
Cause: bmo_voice_gpu container is not running.

ssh misterobots@192.168.2.103
cd ~/Home_AI_Lab/execution_plane
docker compose up -d bmo-voice
docker ps --filter name=bmo_voice_gpu

If it exits immediately, check logs:

docker logs bmo_voice_gpu --tail=30

docs.title: bmo-voice: HTTP 500 on /speak
docs.text: Cause Fix models/bmo.pth not found Copy RVC model to agents/bmo_voice/models/ and rebuild GPU OOM Stop other GPU containers, restart bmo-voice PyTorch weights_only error Already patched — rebuild image to pick up latest server.py

docs.title: Wake-Word: "Hey Beemo" Not Detected
docs.text:
Diagnosis:

# Correct (custom model loaded):
Loaded wake-word models: ['hey_beemo']

# Wrong (fallback — model file missing):
Loaded wake-word models: ['hey_jarvis']

Fix:

# Ensure model is in the working directory
cp agents/bmo_voice/hey_beeMo.onnx ~/bmo_client/

docs.title: Wake-Word: False Positives
docs.text:
Raise the threshold in voice_satellite.py:

THRESHOLD = 0.65   # default 0.5

docs.title: Wake-Word: High Lag / Queue Backup
docs.text:
Symptom: Logs show Q sizes: audio=7+.

The satellite already uses decimation (chunk[::3]) instead of scipy.resample (~100× faster on ARM). If lag persists, reduce chunk size:

CHUNK_SIZE = 640   # 40 ms chunks

docs.title: Microphone: "Device is Busy"
docs.text:
pkill -f voice_satellite.py
pkill -f bmo_driver.py
lsof /dev/snd/*

# Hard reset if still locked:
sudo rmmod snd_usb_audio && sudo modprobe snd_usb_audio

docs.title: Audio Playback: Silence on HDMI
docs.text:
aplay -l                              # Find HDMI card number N
aplay -D plughw:N,0 /usr/share/sounds/alsa/Front_Left.wav   # Test

The satellite already sends a 500 ms silence pre-roll to wake HDMI sinks. If it remains silent, confirm the TV/monitor is on the correct HDMI input.

docs.title: Container: Build Stalls at "#13 exporting layers"
docs.text:
Not a hang

This step writes ~10 GB of CUDA image layers to disk and produces no terminal output (BuildKit uses in-memory progress spinners). Expected duration: 15–25 minutes.

Verify from a second SSH session:

docker ps -a       # Build container still listed
ls -la /var/lib/docker/overlay2/   # New directories appear as layers write

docs.title: Raspberry Pi Service: BMO Doesn't Start on Boot
docs.text:
sudo systemctl status bmo_satellite
journalctl -u bmo_satellite -n 50
Cause Fix LOVELACE_IP not set Add Environment=LOVELACE_IP=192.168.2.103 to service file venv path wrong Check ExecStart= path matches actual venv location Display unavailable Set SDL_VIDEODRIVER=kmsdrm in service environment Pi boots before network Add After=network-online.target to [Unit] 
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart bmo_satellite

docs.title: Emotion Reference
docs.text: Emotion Trigger keywords Pitch Speed excited !, yay, awesome, love, happy +6 st 1.30× happy nice, good, wonderful, fun +3 st 1.15× sad sad, sorry, bad news, oh no −5 st 0.70× surprised whoa, wow, oh my, no way +5 st 1.20× sleeping yawn, sleep, nap, dream −6 st 0.60× thinking ?, hmm, wonder, what if 0 0.90× error error, confused, broken, fail −2 st 0.85× neutral (default) 0 1.00×

docs.title: Pioneer Naming Scheme
docs.text:
All infrastructure components use names from Computing Pioneers. Generic tools and widely-used platforms use Pioneer names as their operational handle. Specialized developer projects retain their original names out of respect for the developers.

Adopted: April 20, 2026

docs.text: Category Rule Example Physical nodes Pioneer name only deploy to Turing, SSH into Hopper Generic infrastructure tools Pioneer container name jacquard (Prometheus), ritchie (Redis) Popular platforms Pioneer container name wozniak (ComfyUI), floyd (Langfuse) Specialized developer projects Retain original name mempalace (MemPalace) Env vars Pioneer prefix + _IP / _HOST HOPPER_IP, TURING_HOST Agent files Pioneer last name church.py, liskov.py

docs.title: Former → Current Quick Reference

docs.title: When IPs or Ports Change
docs.text:
1. Update network.env with new IP addresses.

  2. Update the Network Topology table in Section 1.

  3. Update docker-compose.yml files if port mappings change.

  4. Update jacquard scrape targets in config/jacquard/jacquard.yml.

docs.title: When Services Are Added
docs.text:
1. Add the service to the appropriate node's Service Inventory table.

  2. Add its port to the UI URLs table if it has a web interface.

  3. Add a jacquard scrape target if it exposes metrics.

  4. Add knuth log labels if it produces structured logs.

docs.title: Keeping Environment Variables Current
docs.text:
• After adding new env vars, add them to the Environment Variables table in Section 10.

  • Never commit .env files â document the variable names and purposes only.

docs.title: Manual Verification
docs.text:
1. Network connectivity: Ping each node IP from the Gateway Node.

  2. Service health: curl each API endpoint listed in Section 3 ? verify 200 responses.

  3. jacquard targets: Check http://<gateway-node-ip>:9091/targets ? all should show UP.

  4. hollerith dashboards: Open each dashboard listed in Section 6 ? verify data is flowing.

  5. PostgreSQL schema: Connect to the swarm database ? verify all tables listed in Section 7 exist.

See also: Design Framework · Security · Back to Index

docs.title: Docker Compose Files
docs.text: Each node has its own docker-compose.yml defining the services that run on that node.

docs.title: File Locations
docs.text: Node File Services Control control_plane/docker-compose.yml SPIRE Server, PostgreSQL, Langfuse, ClickHouse, MemPalace, MinIO, Redis Execution execution_plane/docker-compose.yml SPIRE Agent, Ollama, Agent Runtime, ComfyUI, Voice Engine, BMO, OpenHands Gateway turing_gateway/docker-compose.yml SPIRE Agent, Traefik, jacquard, hollerith, knuth, Promtail, AlertManager, Ollama, Redis, Docs Site

docs.title: GPU Access
docs.text:
Services needing GPU access use the NVIDIA runtime:

services:
  ollama:
    deploy:
      resources:
        reservations:
          devices:
            - driver: nvidia
              count: all
              capabilities: [gpu]

docs.title: Volume Mounts
docs.text:
Persistent data uses named volumes:

volumes:
  ollama_data:
  workspace_data:
  postgres_data:

docs.title: Network Configuration
docs.text:
networks:
  ai_lab_net:
    external: true
  internal:
    driver: bridge

docs.title: Environment File
docs.text:
All compose files reference the shared network.env:

docker compose --env-file ../network.env up -d

Or in the compose file:

services:
  agent-runtime:
    env_file:
      - ../network.env

docs.title: Service Dependencies
docs.text:
graph TD
    RT[Agent Runtime] --> Ollama
    RT --> SPIRE-A[SPIRE Agent]
    RT --> PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    RT --> LF[Langfuse]
    RT --> MP[MemPalace]
    LF --> CH[(ClickHouse)]
    LF --> PG
    MP --> PG
    SPIRE-A --> SPIRE-S[SPIRE Server]
    Traefik --> RT
    jacquard --> RT
    jacquard --> Ollama
    Promtail --> knuth
    hollerith --> jacquard
    hollerith --> knuth

docs.title: Useful Commands
docs.text:
# Start all services on a node
docker compose --env-file ../network.env up -d

# View logs for a service
docker compose logs -f agent-runtime

# Restart a single service
docker compose restart agent-runtime

# Rebuild after code changes
docker compose build --no-cache agent-runtime
docker compose up -d agent-runtime

# Stop all services
docker compose down

# Stop and remove volumes (DESTRUCTIVE)
docker compose down -v

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Reference for all variables in network.env.

docs.title: Core Settings
docs.text: Variable Default Description HOPPER_IP 192.168.2.102 Control Plane IP LOVELACE_IP 192.168.2.101 Execution Plane IP TURING_IP 192.168.2.103 Gateway Node IP AGENT_RUNTIME_PORT 8008 Agent Runtime FastAPI port OLLAMA_PORT 11434 Ollama API port

docs.title: Ollama
docs.text: Variable Default Description OLLAMA_HOST 0.0.0.0:11434 Listen address OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL 2 Max concurrent requests OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS 3 Max models in VRAM OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE 10m Idle unload time OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION 1 Enable Flash Attention OLLAMA_ORIGINS * CORS origins

docs.title: Models
docs.text: Variable Default Description SOLVER_MODEL qwen3.6:27b Primary generation model ROUTER_MODEL nemotron-orchestrator:8b Intent classification model VERIFIER_MODEL llama-guard-3:8b Content safety model

docs.title: Database
docs.text: Variable Default Description POSTGRES_USER postgres PostgreSQL username POSTGRES_PASSWORD (secret) PostgreSQL password POSTGRES_DB agent_swarm Database name DATABASE_URL postgresql://... Full connection string

docs.title: Langfuse
docs.text: Variable Default Description LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY (secret) Session encryption key LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY (generated) Public API key LANGFUSE_HOST http://192.168.2.102:3000 Langfuse URL

docs.title: SPIRE
docs.text: Variable Default Description SPIRE_SERVER_ADDRESS 192.168.2.102:8081 SPIRE server endpoint SPIRE_TRUST_DOMAIN home-ai-lab Trust domain name

docs.title: Home Assistant
docs.text: Variable Default Description HA_URL http://192.168.2.100:8123 Home Assistant URL HA_TOKEN (secret) Long-lived access token

docs.title: MinIO
docs.text: Variable Default Description MINIO_ROOT_USER minio MinIO admin username MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD (secret) MinIO admin password MINIO_ENDPOINT 192.168.2.102:9000 MinIO API endpoint

docs.title: Training Dispatcher
docs.text:
Variables that activate and configure the Training Dispatcher on Lovelace.

Variable File Description DISPATCHER_SECRET execution_plane/.env and network.env Shared secret — must match on both Lovelace and Turing. Set in .env so the dispatcher container reads it; set in network.env so agent_runtime reads it to populate the X-Dispatcher-Key header. DISPATCHER_URL network.env Full URL of the dispatcher, e.g. http://192.168.2.101:8001. Read by agent_runtime to know where to send jobs. EXPORT_MIN_SCORE network.env (optional) Minimum MarsRL reward score for trace export. Default: 0.85. HF_TOKEN network.env (optional) HuggingFace token for gated dataset access.

Both files required

DISPATCHER_SECRET must be present in both execution_plane/.env (for the dispatcher container on Lovelace) and network.env (for agent_runtime on Turing). If either is missing, jobs will fail with a 401 or 503 error.

docs.title: Generating the secret
docs.text:
# Windows (PowerShell)
python -c "import secrets; print(secrets.token_hex(32))"

# Linux
openssl rand -hex 32

Use the same output in both files.

docs.title: Activating after setting the secret
docs.text:
# Lovelace — start dispatcher
cd execution_plane && docker compose up -d training-dispatcher

# Turing — restart agent_runtime to pick up new env vars
docker compose up -d --force-recreate agent-runtime

Verify:

curl http://192.168.2.101:8001/health
# {"status":"online","available_archetypes":["coder","coordinator","researcher","creative"],...}

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
All Docker Compose files load network.env via the --env-file flag:

docker compose --env-file ../network.env up -d

Variables are interpolated into compose files:

services:
  agent-runtime:
    environment:
      - OLLAMA_HOST=http://${LOVELACE_IP}:${OLLAMA_PORT}
      - SOLVER_MODEL=${SOLVER_MODEL}

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Admin: Secrets Management — credential security
  • Admin: Docker Compose — compose file reference

docs.title: Models Configuration
docs.text: Model selection, Ollama settings, and custom Modelfiles.

docs.title: Active Models
docs.text: Role Model Parameters VRAM Node Solver qwen3.6:27b 9B ~6 GB Execution Router nemotron-orchestrator:8b 8B ~5 GB Execution Safety Verifier llama-guard-3:8b 8B ~5 GB Execution Vision moondream:latest 1.6B ~2 GB Execution TTS Qwen3-TTS — ~3 GB Execution

docs.title: Pulling Models
docs.text:
docker exec ollama ollama pull qwen3.6:27b
docker exec ollama ollama pull nemotron-orchestrator:8b
docker exec ollama ollama pull llama-guard-3:8b
docker exec ollama ollama pull moondream:latest

docs.title: Memory Management
docs.text: Variable Value Description OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS 3 Max models in VRAM simultaneously OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL 2 Max concurrent inference requests OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE 10m Unload idle models after this time OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION 1 Enable Flash Attention for faster inference

docs.title: VRAM Budget
docs.text:
With RTX 5060 Ti (16 GB VRAM):

Solver (6 GB) + Router (5 GB) + Safety (5 GB) = 16 GB → tight fit

Ollama will auto-unload the least-recently-used model when VRAM is full.

docs.title: Custom Modelfiles
docs.text: Create specializations of base models:

docs.title: Example: Optimized Solver
docs.text:
FROM qwen3.6:27b
PARAMETER temperature 0.7
PARAMETER top_p 0.9
PARAMETER num_ctx 8192
PARAMETER repeat_penalty 1.1
SYSTEM """You are a precise coding assistant. Always:
- Use type hints in Python
- Include error handling
- Add brief inline comments for complex logic"""

docker exec ollama ollama create solver-optimized -f /models/Solver.Modelfile

docs.title: Example: Router with Structured Output
docs.text:
FROM nemotron-orchestrator:8b
PARAMETER temperature 0.3
PARAMETER num_ctx 4096
SYSTEM """You are an intent classifier. Respond ONLY with valid JSON:
{"intent": "...", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "reasoning": "..."}"""

docs.title: Changing Models
docs.text:
1. Update network.env:

    SOLVER_MODEL=qwen3.5:14b

  2. Pull the new model:

    docker exec ollama ollama pull qwen3.5:14b

  3. Restart Agent Runtime:

    docker compose restart agent-runtime

docs.title: Benchmark Reference
docs.text: Model Tokens/sec (RTX 5060 Ti) Context Window qwen3.6:27b ~45 t/s 32K nemotron-orchestrator:8b ~55 t/s 8K llama-guard-3:8b ~60 t/s 8K moondream:latest ~80 t/s 2K

docs.title: A/B Testing
docs.text:
The ExpertiseTemplate system supports A/B testing between models:

# In template registry
"CODE": {
    "model": "qwen3.6:27b",
    "ab_variant": {
        "model": "qwen3.5:14b",
        "traffic_pct": 10  # 10% of requests
    }
}

docs.title: SPIRE Configuration
docs.text: SPIFFE/SPIRE provides workload identity for all Memex services.

docs.title: Server Configuration
docs.text: Location: control_plane/config/spire/server.conf

docs.title: Key Settings
docs.text:
server {

    bind_address = "0.0.0.0"

    bind_port = "8081"

    trust_domain = "home-ai-lab"

    data_dir = "/opt/spire/data/server"

    log_level = "INFO"



    ca_key_type = "ec-p256"

    default_x509_svid_ttl = "8h"

    ca_ttl = "168h"

}



plugins {

    DataStore "sql" {

        plugin_data {

            database_type = "sqlite3"

            connection_string = "/opt/spire/data/server/datastore.sqlite3"

        }

    }



    KeyManager "disk" {

        plugin_data {

            keys_path = "/opt/spire/data/server/keys.json"

        }

    }



    NodeAttestor "join_token" {

        plugin_data {}

    }

}

docs.title: Certificate Lifetimes
docs.text:
| Parameter | Value | Description |

|-----------|-------|-------------|

| default_x509_svid_ttl | 8h | Workload certificate lifetime |

| ca_ttl | 168h (7d) | CA certificate lifetime |

| ca_key_type | ec-p256 | Elliptic curve key type |

docs.title: Agent Configuration
docs.text:
Location: execution_plane/config/spire/agent.conf and turing_gateway/config/spire/agent.conf

agent {

    data_dir = "/opt/spire/data/agent"

    log_level = "INFO"

    server_address = "192.168.2.102"

    server_port = "8081"

    trust_domain = "home-ai-lab"

    socket_path = "/var/run/spire/agent.sock"

}



plugins {

    NodeAttestor "join_token" {

        plugin_data {}

    }



    KeyManager "disk" {

        plugin_data {

            directory = "/opt/spire/data/agent"

        }

    }



    WorkloadAttestor "unix" {

        plugin_data {}

    }

}

docs.title: Workload Registration
docs.text:
Register workloads (services that need identities):

docker compose exec spire-server \

    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server entry create \

    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/agent-runtime \

    -parentID spiffe://home-ai-lab/execution-node \

    -selector unix:uid:1000

docs.title: Current Registrations
docs.text:
| SPIFFE ID | Parent | Selector |

|-----------|--------|----------|

| spiffe://home-ai-lab/agent-runtime | execution-node | unix:uid:1000 |

| spiffe://home-ai-lab/traefik | Turing-gateway | unix:uid:0 |

docs.title: Join Token Generation
docs.text:
# Generate token for Execution Node

docker compose exec spire-server \

    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server token generate \

    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/execution-node \

    -ttl 3600



# Generate token for Gateway Node

docker compose exec spire-server \

    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server token generate \

    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/Turing-gateway \

    -ttl 3600

Token Expiry

Join tokens are single-use and expire after TTL. Generate a fresh token immediately before starting each agent.

docs.title: Health Checks
docs.text:
# Server health

docker compose exec spire-server /opt/spire/bin/spire-server healthcheck



# Agent health

docker compose exec spire-agent /opt/spire/bin/spire-agent healthcheck



# List registered entries

docker compose exec spire-server /opt/spire/bin/spire-server entry show

docs.text:

  • Procedures: Rotate SPIRE Keys  key rotation

docs.title: Traefik Configuration
docs.text: Traefik serves as the reverse proxy and API gateway on the Gateway node (Turing).

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text: Location: turing_gateway/config/traefik/traefik.yml

docs.title: Entry Points
docs.text:
entryPoints:
  web:
    address: ":80"
  websecure:
    address: ":443"

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text:
The Traefik dashboard is available at http://192.168.2.103:8080 for route inspection and debugging.

api:
  dashboard: true
  insecure: true  # LAN-only access

docs.title: Docker Provider
docs.text:
Traefik auto-discovers services via Docker labels:

providers:
  docker:
    endpoint: "unix:///var/run/docker.sock"
    exposedByDefault: false
    network: ai_lab_net

docs.title: Routing via Labels
docs.text: Services declare their routes using Docker labels in docker-compose.yml:

docs.title: Example: Agent Runtime
docs.text:
labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.swarm.rule=PathPrefix(`/swarm`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.swarm.entrypoints=web"
  - "traefik.http.services.swarm.loadbalancer.server.port=8008"
  - "traefik.http.middlewares.swarm-strip.stripprefix.prefixes=/swarm"
  - "traefik.http.routers.swarm.middlewares=swarm-strip"

docs.title: Example: Docs Site
docs.text:
labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.docs.rule=PathPrefix(`/docs`)"
  - "traefik.http.routers.docs.entrypoints=web"
  - "traefik.http.services.docs.loadbalancer.server.port=80"
  - "traefik.http.middlewares.docs-strip.stripprefix.prefixes=/docs"
  - "traefik.http.routers.docs.middlewares=docs-strip"

docs.title: Current Routes
docs.text: Router Rule Service Middleware swarm PathPrefix(/swarm) agent-runtime:8008 strip /swarm comfyui PathPrefix(/comfyui) comfyui:8188 strip /comfyui docs PathPrefix(/docs) docs-site:80 strip /docs hollerith PathPrefix(/hollerith) hollerith:3001 — jacquard PathPrefix(/jacquard) jacquard:9091 —

docs.title: StripPrefix
docs.text:
Removes the path prefix before forwarding to the backend:

Client: GET /swarm/v1/chat/completions
Backend: GET /v1/chat/completions

docs.title: Rate Limiting (Optional)
docs.text:
labels:
  - "traefik.http.middlewares.rate-limit.ratelimit.average=100"
  - "traefik.http.middlewares.rate-limit.ratelimit.burst=200"

docs.title: TLS
docs.text:
For HTTPS with Let's Encrypt:

certificatesResolvers:
  letsencrypt:
    acme:
      email: admin@example.com
      storage: /letsencrypt/acme.json
      httpChallenge:
        entryPoint: web

LAN-Only

Currently running HTTP-only within the LAN. TLS is handled by Tailscale for remote access.

docs.title: Deploy Control Plane
docs.text: The Control Plane (Hopper, 192.168.2.102) runs identity, databases, tracing, and memory services.

docs.title: Services
docs.text: Service Port Purpose SPIRE Server 8081 Workload identity CA PostgreSQL 5432 Primary database (pgvector) Langfuse 3000 LLM tracing ClickHouse 8123 Langfuse analytics backend MemPalace 8200 Semantic memory store MinIO 9000/9001 Object storage Redis 6379 Cache

docs.title: 1. Prepare the Node
docs.text:
ssh user@192.168.2.102
cd /opt/Agent_Swarm
git pull origin main

docs.title: 2. Configure Environment
docs.text:
Copy and edit the environment file:

cp network.env.example network.env
nano network.env

Key variables for the Control Plane:

Variable Example Description POSTGRES_PASSWORD (generate strong password) PostgreSQL admin password LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY (generate random key) Langfuse session secret MINIO_ROOT_USER minio MinIO admin user MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD (generate strong password) MinIO admin password

Credential Security

Never commit network.env to Git. It contains secrets. Use .gitignore to exclude it.

docs.title: 3. Start Services
docs.text:
cd control_plane
docker compose --env-file ../network.env up -d

docs.title: 4. Initialize SPIRE Server
docs.text:
# Check SPIRE server is running
docker compose exec spire-server /opt/spire/bin/spire-server healthcheck

# Generate join tokens for agents
docker compose exec spire-server \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server token generate \
    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/execution-node \
    -ttl 3600

Save the generated token — you'll need it when deploying the Execution Node.

docs.title: 5. Verify Services
docs.text:
# PostgreSQL
docker compose exec postgres pg_isready

# Langfuse
curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/public/health | jq .

# MemPalace
curl -s http://localhost:8200/health

# MinIO
curl -s http://localhost:9000/minio/health/live

docs.title: 6. Initialize Database
docs.text:
# Create pgvector extension
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c "CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;"

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Solution SPIRE healthcheck fails Check docker compose logs spire-server for config errors PostgreSQL won't start Check disk space, verify POSTGRES_PASSWORD is set Langfuse 500 errors Ensure ClickHouse is running: docker compose logs clickhouse MemPalace can't connect to PG Verify PostgreSQL is up and POSTGRES_PASSWORD matches

docs.title: Next
docs.text: → Deploy Execution Plane

docs.title: Deploy Execution Plane
docs.text: The Execution Plane (Lovelace, 192.168.2.101) runs all GPU compute: Ollama, Agent Runtime, ComfyUI, Voice Engine, and sandboxed code execution.

docs.title: Services
docs.text: Service Port GPU Purpose Ollama 11434 ✅ LLM inference Agent Runtime 8008 — FastAPI agent orchestrator ComfyUI 8188 ✅ Image generation pipelines Voice Engine 5050 ✅ TTS (Qwen3-TTS + RVC) BMO Voice 5060 ✅ Character voice assistant OpenHands 3300 — Sandboxed code execution SPIRE Agent — — Workload identity

docs.title: 1. Prepare the Node
docs.text:
cd C:\Users\panca\Documents\Github\Agent_Swarm
git pull origin main

Ensure Docker Desktop is running with WSL 2 backend and GPU support enabled.

docs.title: 2. Verify GPU Access
docs.text:
# Check NVIDIA driver
nvidia-smi

# Check Docker GPU access
docker run --rm --gpus all nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-base-ubuntu22.04 nvidia-smi

docs.title: 3. Pull Models
docs.text:
# Start Ollama first
docker compose -f execution_plane/docker-compose.yml up -d ollama

# Pull required models
docker exec ollama ollama pull qwen3.6:27b
docker exec ollama ollama pull nemotron-orchestrator:8b
docker exec ollama ollama pull llama-guard-3:8b
docker exec ollama ollama pull moondream:latest

Model Download

Initial model pulls may take 10–30 minutes depending on bandwidth. Models are cached in the Ollama Docker volume.

docs.title: 4. Configure SPIRE Agent
docs.text:
Set the join token from the Control Plane deployment:

# Edit the agent configuration
notepad execution_plane\config\spire\agent.conf

Update join_token with the token generated during Control Plane setup.

docs.title: 5. Start All Services
docs.text:
cd execution_plane
docker compose --env-file ..\network.env up -d

docs.title: 6. Verify Services
docs.text:
# Agent Runtime health
curl http://localhost:8008/

# Ollama models loaded
curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags | python -m json.tool

# ComfyUI
curl http://localhost:8188/system_stats

# SPIRE agent health
docker compose exec spire-agent /opt/spire/bin/spire-agent healthcheck

docs.title: 7. Ollama Configuration
docs.text:
Key Ollama environment variables in docker-compose.yml:

Variable Recommended Description OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL 2 Max concurrent requests OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS 3 Max models in VRAM OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE 10m Idle model unload time OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION 1 Enable Flash Attention

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Solution nvidia-smi not found Install/update NVIDIA drivers docker: Error response from daemon: could not select device driver Install NVIDIA Container Toolkit Ollama OOM Reduce OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS, or pull smaller quant Agent Runtime can't reach Ollama Check OLLAMA_HOST in network.env SPIRE agent attestation failed Regenerate join token from Control Plane

docs.title: Next
docs.text: → Deploy Gateway

docs.title: Deploy Gateway
docs.text: The Gateway (Turing, 192.168.2.103) runs Traefik reverse proxy, the monitoring stack, and secondary Ollama.

docs.title: Services
docs.text: Service Port Purpose Traefik 80, 443, 8080 Reverse proxy, TLS termination jacquard 9091 Metrics collection hollerith 3001 Dashboards knuth 3100 Log aggregation Promtail — Log shipper AlertManager 9093 Alert routing Ollama (secondary) 11435 Overflow inference cAdvisor 8080 Container metrics Redis 6379 Cache, queues SPIRE Agent — Workload identity

docs.title: 1. Prepare the Node
docs.text:
ssh user@192.168.2.103
cd /opt/Agent_Swarm
git pull origin main

docs.title: 2. Configure Traefik
docs.text:
Review and update turing_gateway/config/traefik/traefik.yml:

entryPoints:
  web:
    address: ":80"
  websecure:
    address: ":443"

providers:
  docker:
    endpoint: "unix:///var/run/docker.sock"
    exposedByDefault: false
    network: ai_lab_net

docs.title: 3. Configure SPIRE Agent
docs.text:
Set the join token from Control Plane:

nano turing_gateway/config/spire/agent.conf

docs.title: 4. Create External Networks
docs.text:
docker network create ai_lab_net 2>/dev/null || true
docker network create saltbox 2>/dev/null || true

docs.title: 5. Start All Services
docs.text:
cd turing_gateway
docker compose --env-file ../network.env up -d

docs.title: 6. Verify Services
docs.text:
# Traefik dashboard
curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/overview | jq .

# jacquard targets
curl -s http://localhost:9091/api/v1/targets | jq '.data.activeTargets | length'

# hollerith
curl -s http://localhost:3001/api/health

# knuth
curl -s http://localhost:3100/ready

# AlertManager
curl -s http://localhost:9093/-/healthy

docs.title: 7. Configure hollerith Data Sources
docs.text:
On first deployment, add data sources in hollerith at http://192.168.2.103:3001:

  1. jacquard: URL = http://jacquard:9091
  2. knuth: URL = http://knuth:3100

Import dashboards from turing_gateway/config/hollerith/dashboards/.

docs.title: Traefik Routing Rules
docs.text: Path Target Notes /swarm/* Agent Runtime (192.168.2.101:8008) Main API /comfyui/* ComfyUI (192.168.2.101:8188) Image generation UI /docs/* Docs Site (localhost:80) This documentation /hollerith/* hollerith (localhost:3001) Monitoring dashboards /jacquard/* jacquard (localhost:9091) Metrics

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Solution Traefik 502 Backend service down — check target container logs Traefik 404 Route not matched — check PathPrefix labels jacquard targets down Verify target IPs and ports in jacquard.yml hollerith "No data" Check data source configuration and time range cAdvisor high CPU Normal during initial scan; settles after ~2 min

docs.title: Next
docs.text: → Networking

docs.title: Networking
docs.text: Network topology, DNS, remote access, and firewall configuration.

docs.title: LAN Layout
docs.text:
graph LR
    Router["Router<br/>192.168.2.1"] --- HA["Home Assistant<br/>192.168.2.100"]
    Router --- Exec["Lovelace<br/>192.168.2.101"]
    Router --- Ctrl["Hopper<br/>192.168.2.102"]
    Router --- GW["Turing<br/>192.168.2.103"]
    Router --- iDRAC["iDRAC<br/>192.168.2.104"]

All nodes sit on a single flat 192.168.2.0/24 subnet.

docs.title: Static IP Assignments
docs.text:
Node IP MAC (example) Home Assistant 192.168.2.100 (DHCP reservation) Lovelace (Exec) 192.168.2.101 (DHCP reservation) Hopper (Ctrl) 192.168.2.102 (DHCP reservation) Turing (Gateway) 192.168.2.103 (DHCP reservation) iDRAC 192.168.2.104 (static)

DHCP Reservations

Configure IP reservations in your router instead of static IPs on each host. This keeps network config centralized.

docs.title: Docker Networks
docs.text:
Each node uses Docker Compose networks. Cross-node communication uses host IPs.

Network Node Purpose ai_lab_net Gateway Traefik ↔ local services saltbox Gateway Shared with Saltbox media stack control_net Control Internal control plane services exec_net Execution Internal execution plane services

docs.title: Remote Access (Tailscale)
docs.text:
Tailscale provides encrypted remote access without port forwarding.

Property Value Tailnet DNS *.tail*.ts.net Enabled Nodes Turing, Lovelace MagicDNS Enabled

Access services remotely:

http://Turing.tail12345.ts.net/swarm/v1/chat/completions
http://Turing.tail12345.ts.net/hollerith

Tailscale Setup

Install Tailscale on nodes that need remote access. No router port forwarding needed.

docs.title: Required Ports Between Nodes
docs.text: Source Destination Port Protocol Purpose Gateway Execution 8008 TCP Agent Runtime Gateway Execution 11434 TCP Ollama Gateway Execution 8188 TCP ComfyUI Execution Control 8081 TCP SPIRE Execution Control 5432 TCP PostgreSQL Execution Control 3000 TCP Langfuse Execution Control 8200 TCP MemPalace Gateway Control 5432 TCP PostgreSQL (hollerith) All Gateway 80, 443 TCP Traefik

docs.title: External Access
docs.text:
Only the Gateway node exposes ports externally (via Tailscale or LAN):

Port Service Access 80 Traefik HTTP LAN + Tailscale 443 Traefik HTTPS LAN + Tailscale 8080 Traefik Dashboard LAN only

docs.title: DNS
docs.text:
No custom DNS required. Services reference each other by IP address in network.env. Local names are resolved via:

  • Docker internal DNS (within compose networks)
  • Tailscale MagicDNS (for remote access)
  • /etc/hosts entries (optional, for convenience)

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture: Topology — physical layout details
  • Reference: Port Map — complete port registry
  • Troubleshooting: Network — connectivity issues

docs.title: Post-Deployment Verification
docs.text: Smoke tests and verification steps after deploying all three nodes.

docs.title: Pre-Checks
docs.text:
Ensure all three nodes are deployed:

  • Control Plane services are running
  • Execution Plane services are running
  • Gateway services are running

docs.title: 1. End-to-End Connectivity
docs.text:
# From Gateway, verify execution node
curl -s http://192.168.2.101:8008/ | jq .

# From Execution, verify control plane
curl -s http://192.168.2.102:3000/api/public/health | jq .
curl -s http://192.168.2.102:8200/health

docs.title: 2. SPIRE Identity Chain
docs.text:
# On Execution node
docker compose exec spire-agent \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-agent healthcheck

# Should show: Agent is healthy

docs.title: 3. Model Availability
docs.text:
curl -s http://192.168.2.101:11434/api/tags | \
    python -c "import sys,json; [print(m['name']) for m in json.load(sys.stdin)['models']]"

Expected models:

  • qwen3.6:27b
  • nemotron-orchestrator:8b
  • llama-guard-3:8b
  • moondream:latest

docs.title: 4. First Chat Request
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103/swarm/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello, are you working?"}],
        "stream": false
    }'

Expected: A response with "intent": "CONVERSATION" and a friendly answer.

docs.title: 5. Image Generation
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103/swarm/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Generate an image of a sunset"}],
        "stream": false
    }'

Check that ComfyUI executes and an image path is returned.

docs.title: 6. Monitoring Stack
docs.text: Check URL Expected hollerith http://192.168.2.103:3001 Login page jacquard http://192.168.2.103:9091/targets All targets UP Langfuse http://192.168.2.102:3000 Traces from step 4

docs.title: 7. Traefik Routes
docs.text:
curl -s http://192.168.2.103:8080/api/http/routers | \
    python -c "import sys,json; [print(r['rule']) for r in json.load(sys.stdin)]"

Should list PathPrefix rules for /swarm, /comfyui, /docs, /hollerith.

docs.title: Common First-Run Issues
docs.text: Symptom Cause Fix 502 on /swarm/* Agent Runtime not started Check execution node containers Empty model list Models not pulled Run ollama pull for each model Langfuse "connection refused" ClickHouse not ready Wait 30s, restart Langfuse SPIRE attestation failure Expired join token Generate new token from Control Plane jacquard target down Wrong IP in jacquard.yml Update scrape config IPs

docs.title: What's Next
docs.text:
Once verification passes:

  1. Import hollerith dashboards (see Monitoring)
  2. Review alert rules (see Operations: Alerts)
  3. Set up backup schedule (see Backup & Restore)
  4. Onboard users (see Getting Started: User Quickstart)

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Hardware and software requirements for deploying Memex.

docs.title: Minimum (Single Node)
docs.text: Component Requirement CPU 8 cores, x86-64 RAM 32 GB GPU NVIDIA with ≥ 12 GB VRAM (CUDA 12+) Storage 200 GB SSD Network 1 Gbps LAN

docs.title: Recommended (3-Node)
docs.text: Node Role CPU RAM GPU Storage Execution GPU compute 8+ cores 32 GB RTX 5060 Ti 16 GB+ 500 GB SSD Control Databases 4 cores 8 GB None 200 GB SSD Gateway Proxy, monitoring 8+ cores 64 GB RTX 3070 Ti 8 GB (optional) 500 GB SSD

docs.title: All Nodes
docs.text: Software Version Purpose Docker Engine 24.0+ Container runtime Docker Compose v2.20+ Service orchestration Git 2.30+ Repository cloning

docs.title: Execution Node (Windows)
docs.text: Software Version Purpose Docker Desktop 4.25+ Docker on Windows NVIDIA Driver 550+ GPU access NVIDIA Container Toolkit Latest GPU passthrough to containers WSL 2 Ubuntu 22.04 Linux subsystem for Docker

docs.title: Control & Gateway Nodes (Linux)
docs.text: Software Version Purpose Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Host OS NVIDIA Driver 550+ GPU access (Gateway only) NVIDIA Container Toolkit Latest GPU passthrough (Gateway only)

docs.title: VRAM Allocation (Reference)
docs.text:
Model VRAM Node qwen3.6:27b ~6 GB Execution nemotron-orchestrator:8b ~5 GB Execution llama-guard-3:8b ~5 GB Execution FLUX.1-schnell (ComfyUI) ~10 GB Execution Qwen3-TTS ~3 GB Execution Moondream2 (VLM) ~2 GB Execution

VRAM Management

Not all models fit in VRAM simultaneously. Ollama manages model loading/unloading automatically. Set OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=2 and OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=3 to control memory pressure.

docs.title: Network
docs.text:
• All nodes on the same LAN subnet (e.g., 192.168.2.0/24)
  • Static IP addresses assigned to each node
  • Ports 80, 443, 8000–9100 open between nodes (see Port Map)
  • Internet access for model pulling and package installation

docs.title: Pre-Deployment Checklist
docs.text:
• All nodes powered on and accessible via SSH/RDP
  • Docker installed and running on all nodes
  • NVIDIA drivers installed on GPU nodes
  • nvidia-smi shows GPU on GPU nodes
  • docker run --gpus all nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-base-ubuntu22.04 nvidia-smi succeeds on GPU nodes
  • Nodes can ping each other by IP
  • Repository cloned: git clone https://github.com/your-org/Agent_Swarm.git
  • network.env populated with correct values

docs.title: Next
docs.text: → Deploy Control Plane

docs.title: Backup & Restore
docs.text: Strategies for backing up and recovering Memex data.

docs.title: What to Back Up
docs.text:
| Data | Location | Method | Frequency |

|------|----------|--------|-----------|

| PostgreSQL | Control Node | pg_dump | Daily |

| Skills Memory | Execution Node volume | File copy | Daily |

| User Projects | Execution Node volume | tar/rsync | Daily |

| Delivered Artifacts | Execution Node volume | tar/rsync | Weekly |

| Training Data | Execution Node volume | tar/rsync | Weekly |

| Docker Compose files | Git repository | git push | On change |

| Config files | Git repository | git push | On change |

| hollerith dashboards | Gateway Node | API export | On change |

Not Backed Up

  • Ollama model cache (re-pullable)

  • jacquard TSDB (retention-based, ephemeral)

  • Container images (re-pullable from registry)

docs.title: PostgreSQL Backup
docs.text:
# On Control Node

docker compose exec postgres pg_dump -U postgres -Fc > /backups/pg_$(date +%Y%m%d).dump



# Restore

docker compose exec -T postgres pg_restore -U postgres -d postgres --clean < /backups/pg_20260410.dump

docs.title: Automated Daily Backup
docs.text:
Add to crontab on Control Node:

0 3 * * * docker compose -f /opt/Agent_Swarm/control_plane/docker-compose.yml exec -T postgres pg_dump -U postgres -Fc > /backups/pg_$(date +\%Y\%m\%d).dump

docs.title: Skills Memory and Sessions
docs.text:
# On Execution Node

docker compose exec agent-runtime tar czf /tmp/memory-backup.tar.gz \

    /workspace/agents/skills_memory.json \

    /workspace/agents/context_sessions/



docker cp agent-runtime:/tmp/memory-backup.tar.gz /backups/

docs.title: User Projects and Artifacts
docs.text:
# Tar the workspace volumes

docker compose exec agent-runtime tar czf /tmp/workspace-backup.tar.gz \

    /workspace/user_projects/ \

    /workspace/delivered_artifacts/ \

    /workspace/training_data/

docs.title: Full System Backup Script
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash

BACKUP_DIR="/backups/$(date +%Y%m%d)"

mkdir -p "$BACKUP_DIR"



echo "Backing up PostgreSQL..."

ssh user@192.168.2.102 \

    "docker compose -f /opt/Agent_Swarm/control_plane/docker-compose.yml exec -T postgres pg_dump -U postgres -Fc" \

    > "$BACKUP_DIR/postgres.dump"



echo "Backing up workspace volumes..."

ssh user@192.168.2.101 \

    "docker compose -f /opt/Agent_Swarm/execution_plane/docker-compose.yml exec -T agent-runtime tar czf - /workspace" \

    > "$BACKUP_DIR/workspace.tar.gz"



echo "Backup complete: $BACKUP_DIR"

ls -lh "$BACKUP_DIR"

docs.title: Full Restore
docs.text:
1. Deploy fresh nodes per the Deployment Guide

  2. Restore PostgreSQL: pg_restore as shown above

  3. Restore workspace volumes: tar xzf workspace.tar.gz

  4. Re-pull models: ollama pull qwen3.6:27b, etc.

  5. Verify: Run Post-Deployment Checks

docs.title: Partial Restore (Skills Memory Only)
docs.text:
docker cp memory-backup.tar.gz agent-runtime:/tmp/

docker compose exec agent-runtime tar xzf /tmp/memory-backup.tar.gz -C /

docs.title: Retention Policy
docs.text:
| Data | Retention | Storage |

|------|-----------|---------|

| PostgreSQL dumps | 30 days | Local + offsite |

| Workspace snapshots | 14 days | Local |

| Artifact backups | 90 days | Local + MinIO |

docs.text:

  • Architecture: Memory System  what's stored where

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text: Dashboards, alerts, and health checks for Memex.

docs.title: Dashboards
docs.text: Access hollerith at http://192.168.2.103:3001.

docs.title: Pre-Built Dashboards
docs.text:
| Dashboard | Description |

|-----------|-------------|

| System Overview | Node health, container status, CPU/memory/disk |

| Agent Performance | Request throughput, MarsRL scores, intent distribution |

| GPU Utilization | VRAM usage, model loading times, inference rates |

| Ollama Metrics | Model stats, request queue, token generation speed |

| Alert Status | Active alerts and history |

docs.title: Importing Dashboards
docs.text:
# Copy dashboard JSON files to hollerith provisioning

cp turing_gateway/config/hollerith/dashboards/*.json \

    /var/lib/hollerith/dashboards/

Or import manually: hollerith ? Dashboards ? Import ? Upload JSON.

docs.title: Quick Status Script
docs.text:
#!/bin/bash

echo "=== Control Plane ==="

curl -sf http://192.168.2.102:3000/api/public/health && echo "Langfuse: OK" || echo "Langfuse: DOWN"

curl -sf http://192.168.2.102:8200/health && echo "MemPalace: OK" || echo "MemPalace: DOWN"



echo "=== Execution Plane ==="

curl -sf http://192.168.2.101:8008/ && echo "Runtime: OK" || echo "Runtime: DOWN"

curl -sf http://192.168.2.101:11434/api/tags > /dev/null && echo "Ollama: OK" || echo "Ollama: DOWN"



echo "=== Gateway ==="

curl -sf http://192.168.2.103:3001/api/health && echo "hollerith: OK" || echo "hollerith: DOWN"

curl -sf http://192.168.2.103:9091/-/healthy && echo "jacquard: OK" || echo "jacquard: DOWN"

curl -sf http://192.168.2.103:3100/ready && echo "knuth: OK" || echo "knuth: DOWN"

docs.title: jacquard Targets
docs.text:
Check all scrape targets: http://192.168.2.103:9091/targets

All targets should show UP state. Down targets indicate connectivity issues.

docs.title: Alerts
docs.text: AlertManager at http://192.168.2.103:9093 routes alerts to email and ntfy.

docs.title: Default Alert Rules
docs.text:
| Alert | Condition | Severity |

|-------|-----------|----------|

| NodeDown | Node unreachable for 2 min | Critical |

| HighCPU | CPU > 90% for 5 min | Warning |

| HighMemory | Memory > 85% for 5 min | Warning |

| OllamaOOM | Ollama container OOM | Critical |

| DiskFull | Disk > 90% full | Critical |

| HighErrorRate | API 5xx > 5% for 5 min | Warning |

| SlowResponse | p95 latency > 30s for 5 min | Warning |

docs.title: Alert Configuration
docs.text:
Edit alert rules in turing_gateway/config/jacquard/alert_rules.yml.

AlertManager routing in turing_gateway/config/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml:

route:

  receiver: 'default'

  routes:

    - match:

        severity: critical

      receiver: 'critical-email'

    - match:

        severity: warning

      receiver: 'ntfy-push'

docs.title: Key Metrics to Watch
docs.text:
| Metric | Good | Investigate |

|--------|------|-------------|

| Agent Runtime response time (p95) | < 10s | > 30s |

| MarsRL pass rate (first try) | > 80% | < 60% |

| Ollama VRAM usage | < 90% | > 95% |

| Disk usage | < 80% | > 90% |

| Error rate (5xx) | < 1% | > 5% |

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture: Observability  full stack details

  • Procedures: Configure Alerting  alert setup

  • Troubleshooting  common issues

docs.title: Scaling
docs.text: Strategies for adding capacity to Memex.

docs.title: Add GPU VRAM
docs.text:
Upgrade the GPU on the Execution Node to load more models simultaneously:

| GPU | VRAM | Concurrent Models |

|-----|------|--------------------|

| RTX 3070 Ti | 8 GB | 12 |

| RTX 5060 Ti | 16 GB | 23 |

| RTX 4090 | 24 GB | 34 |

| A100 | 80 GB | 8+ |

After GPU upgrade:

# Increase Ollama limits

# In docker-compose.yml:

OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=4

OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=4

docs.title: Add RAM
docs.text: The Gateway node benefits from more RAM for jacquard TSDB and knuth retention.

docs.title: Add a Second GPU Node
docs.text:
1. Deploy a new node following Execution Plane

  2. Register with SPIRE:

# On Control Node

docker compose exec spire-server \

    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server token generate \

    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/execution-node-2
  1. Add Traefik load balancing in Gateway:
# Weighted round-robin across execution nodes

services:

  agent-runtime:

    loadBalancer:

      servers:

        - url: "http://192.168.2.101:8008"

        - url: "http://192.168.2.105:8008"

docs.title: Ollama Load Balancing
docs.text:
For inference-heavy workloads, add a secondary Ollama on the Gateway node (already configured as ollama-secondary on port 11435):

# In config, the router can fallback to secondary Ollama

OLLAMA_HOSTS = [

    "http://192.168.2.101:11434",  # primary (RTX 5060 Ti)

    "http://192.168.2.103:11435",                 # secondary (RTX 3070 Ti)

]

docs.title: Queue-Based Scaling
docs.text:
The Dispatcher already supports concurrent worker limits per queue:

| Queue | Current Workers | Max Practical |

|-------|----------------|---------------|

| default (chat) | 5 | 10 |

| image | 2 | 4 (VRAM limited) |

| 3d | 1 | 2 (VRAM limited) |

| action_figure | 1 | 1 |

Increase workers in agents/dispatcher.py configuration.

docs.title: MinIO for Artifacts
docs.text:
If local disk fills up, offload artifacts to MinIO (Control Node):

http://192.168.2.102:9000

docs.title: jacquard Retention
docs.text:
Adjust retention in turing_gateway/docker-compose.yml:

command:

  - '--storage.tsdb.retention.time=90d'

  - '--storage.tsdb.retention.size=20GB'

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture: Topology  current node layout

  • Admin: Prerequisites  hardware recommendations

docs.title: Secrets Management
docs.text: How credentials are managed across Memex nodes.

docs.title: Secret Categories
docs.text: Category Examples Storage Database PostgreSQL password, MinIO credentials network.env API Keys Langfuse keys, Authentik tokens network.env SPIRE Join tokens, trust bundle SPIRE server (ephemeral) OAuth Client secrets, redirect URIs Authentik config TLS Certificates, private keys Traefik auto-managed

docs.title: network.env
docs.text:
The network.env file at the repository root contains all shared secrets. This file is not committed to Git (listed in .gitignore).

Security

  • Never commit network.env to version control
  • Restrict file permissions: chmod 600 network.env
  • Use a password manager to store a backup
  • Rotate secrets periodically

docs.title: Structure
docs.text:
# Database
POSTGRES_PASSWORD=<strong-random-password>

# Langfuse
LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=<random-key>

# MinIO
MINIO_ROOT_USER=minio
MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD=<strong-random-password>

# OAuth (Authentik)
AUTHENTIK_SECRET_KEY=<random-key>

docs.title: SPIRE Credentials
docs.text:
SPIRE uses ephemeral join tokens instead of static credentials:

  1. Tokens are generated on the SPIRE Server
  2. Each token is single-use
  3. Tokens expire after TTL (default: 3600s)
  4. After attestation, the agent receives an X.509 SVID

See Procedure: Rotate SPIRE Keys for rotation.

docs.title: Rotation Schedule
docs.text: Secret Rotation Procedure PostgreSQL password Quarterly Update network.env, restart PG Langfuse keys Quarterly Update network.env, restart Langfuse SPIRE join tokens Per-use Generated fresh each time OAuth client secrets Annually Regenerate in Authentik TLS certificates Auto Traefik ACME auto-renewal

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture: Security Model
  • Procedures: Rotate SPIRE Keys
  • Configuration: Environment

docs.title: Updates
docs.text: How to update models, container images, and Memex code.

docs.title: Pull New Model Version
docs.text:
# On Execution Node
docker exec ollama ollama pull qwen3.6:27b
docker exec ollama ollama pull nemotron-orchestrator:8b

docs.title: Switch Models
docs.text:
Update network.env with new model names:

SOLVER_MODEL=qwen3.5:14b
ROUTER_MODEL=nemotron-orchestrator:12b

Then restart the Agent Runtime:

docker compose restart agent-runtime

docs.title: Custom Modelfiles
docs.text:
For fine-tuned models, create a Modelfile:

FROM qwen3.5:9b
PARAMETER temperature 0.7
PARAMETER top_p 0.9
SYSTEM "You are a helpful assistant."

docker exec ollama ollama create custom-solver -f /models/Modelfile

docs.title: Update Agent Runtime
docs.text:
cd execution_plane
docker compose pull agent-runtime
docker compose up -d agent-runtime

docs.title: Update All Services on a Node
docs.text:
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d

Rolling Updates

Update one node at a time. Verify health after each node update before proceeding to the next.

docs.title: Code Updates
docs.text:
git pull origin main
docker compose build --no-cache agent-runtime
docker compose up -d

docs.title: Container Rollback
docs.text:
# List image history

docker images --format "table {{.Repository}}\t{{.Tag}}\t{{.CreatedAt}}" | head -20


# Revert to previous image
docker compose up -d --no-deps agent-runtime

docs.title: Code Rollback
docs.text:
git log --oneline -10
git checkout <previous-commit>
docker compose build agent-runtime
docker compose up -d

docs.title: Update Checklist
docs.text:
• Notify users of planned maintenance
  • Back up databases (Backup & Restore)
  • Pull new images/models
  • Update one node at a time
  • Verify health after each update
  • Check Langfuse traces for errors
  • Monitor hollerith for anomalies

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Technical design documentation for the Memex system.


graph TB

    subgraph Gateway["Gateway Node · Turing · 192.168.2.103"]

        Traefik[Traefik]

        jacquard[jacquard]

        hollerith[hollerith]

        knuth[knuth]

        OllamaGW[Ollama Secondary]

    end



    subgraph Execution["Execution Node · Lovelace · 192.168.2.101"]

        Runtime[Agent Runtime]

        Ollama[Ollama Primary]

        ComfyUI[ComfyUI]

        VoiceEng[Voice Engine]

        OpenHands[OpenHands]

    end



    subgraph Control["Control Node · Hopper · 192.168.2.102"]

        SPIRE[SPIRE Server]

        PG[(PostgreSQL)]

        Langfuse[Langfuse]

        CH[(ClickHouse)]

        MemPalace[MemPalace]

    end



    Traefik -->|/swarm/*| Runtime

    Runtime --> Ollama

    Runtime --> ComfyUI

    Runtime --> VoiceEng

    Runtime -.->|identity| SPIRE

    Runtime -.->|traces| Langfuse

    Runtime -.->|memory| MemPalace

    jacquard -->|scrape| Runtime

docs.title: Sections
docs.text:
| Section | Description |

|---------|-------------|

| Topology | Physical 3-node layout, hardware, network |

| Data Flow | Request lifecycle from user input to response |

| MarsRL Loop | Inference-time quality verification |

| Security Model | SPIFFE/SPIRE, JWT-ACE, MAESTRO |

| Memory System | Persistent knowledge, preferences, MemPalace |

| Architecture Decisions | ADR index and records |

docs.title: Agent System
docs.text: How Memex classifies user intent, selects the right agent, and orchestrates complex tasks.

docs.title: Semantic Router
docs.text: The router is the "frontal cortex" of the system. It uses nemotron-orchestrator:8b to classify every user message into one of 14 intents.

docs.title: Intent Categories
docs.text: Intent Description Routes To CONVERSATION Chat, questions, small talk MarsRL Loop CODE Programming, debugging, scripts MarsRL Loop DEVOPS Docker, CI/CD, Linux MarsRL Loop DATA SQL, analytics, CSV, stats MarsRL Loop IMAGE 2D art, photos, illustrations Image Agent → ComfyUI 3D 3D models, meshes, geometry 3D Pipeline ACTION_FIGURE Posable figure design Action Figure Agent RESEARCH Deep analysis, comparison Coordinator DOCUMENTATION Writing, reformatting, summaries MarsRL Loop TRAIN Teaching preferences / rules Memory System IOT_CONTROL Smart home device control IoT Agent → Home Assistant IOT_DEV Firmware, circuits, MQTT IoT Dev Agent VISION Analyzing images Vision Agent (Moondream) COORDINATE Complex multi-step tasks Coordinator

docs.title: Classification Flow
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[User Message] --> B[Semantic Router]
    B -->|"Nemotron-8B"| C{Confidence}
    C -->|"≥ 0.60"| D[Accept Intent]
    C -->|"< 0.60 or AMBIGUOUS"| E[Retry with stronger prompt]
    E --> C
    C -->|"Timeout/failure"| F[Fallback: CONVERSATION]
    D --> G[Route to Agent]

docs.title: Router Output
docs.text:
{
    "intent": "CODE",
    "confidence": 0.92,
    "reasoning": "User requested a Python function implementation",
    "disambiguation_question": null
}

docs.title: Core Agents
docs.text: Agent File Model Purpose Solver / Architect architect_agent.py qwen3.6:27b Primary response generation Verifier verifier_agent.py Multi-layer Output validation Corrector corrector_agent.py qwen3.6:27b Error correction Security Agent security_agent.py — Command safety, blocklist enforcement

docs.title: Specialized Agents
docs.text: Agent File Purpose Image Gen specialized/image_gen.py ComfyUI pipeline orchestration BMO Agent specialized/bmo_agent.py BMO character voice personality IoT Agent specialized/iot_agent.py Home Assistant device control Voice Assistant specialized/voice_assistant.py Voice interaction handler Action Figure specialized/action_figure_agent.py Articulated figure design Forge Agent specialized/forge_agent.py 3D model reconstruction

docs.title: Coordinator
docs.text: The Coordinator handles COORDINATE and RESEARCH intents by orchestrating multiple agents in parallel.

docs.title: Phases
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[User Task] --> B[Decompose]
    B --> C[Research · Parallel]
    C --> D[Synthesize]
    D --> E[Implement]
    E --> F[Verify]
Phase Description Decompose LLM breaks the task into subtasks Research Parallel workers investigate unknowns Synthesize Merge findings into a coherent plan Implement Execute the plan with appropriate agents Verify Fresh worker validates the results

docs.title: Worker Roles
docs.text:
# Role → Agent mapping
"architect" → Architect Agent
"coder"     → Architect Agent  
"devops"    → Architect Agent
"analyst"   → Data Analyst Agent
"researcher"→ Research Worker
"verifier"  → Verification Worker

Workers communicate via a shared scratchpad (filesystem-based) for intermediate artifacts.

docs.title: ExpertiseTemplate System
docs.text:
The template registry controls which model, parameters, and tools are used for each intent:

graph LR
    A[Intent: CODE] --> B[Template Registry]
    B --> C[Model: qwen3.5:9b]
    B --> D[Tools: file_ops, terminal]
    B --> E[Params: temp=0.7, top_p=0.9]
    B --> F[A/B Variant: 10% traffic]

Templates support:

  • Default models per intent
  • A/B testing with percentage-based traffic splitting
  • Version tracking for rollback
  • Parameter overrides per template

docs.title: Key Files
docs.text: File Purpose agents/semantic_router.py Intent classification using Nemotron agents/router.py Request handling, token issuance, agent dispatch agents/coordinator.py Multi-worker orchestration agents/mars_loop.py MarsRL verification pipeline agents/expertise/template_registry.py Model/parameter resolution agents/intent_capabilities.py Intent → capability mapping

docs.text:

  • Architecture: MarsRL — quality verification details

  • Module: Coordinator — orchestration details
  • Developer Guide: Adding Agents — create new agents

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text: How a request travels from user input to final response.

docs.title: Full Request Lifecycle
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant UI as Hive UI
    participant GW as Traefik (Gateway)
    participant RT as Agent Runtime
    participant SR as Semantic Router
    participant TI as Token Issuer
    participant Sol as Solver (Ollama)
    participant Ver as Verifier
    participant Cor as Corrector
    participant LF as Langfuse

    User->>UI: Enter message
    UI->>GW: POST /swarm/v1/chat/completions
    GW->>RT: Forward to execution node

    RT->>SR: Classify intent
    SR->>Sol: Nemotron-8B analysis
    Sol-->>SR: {intent, confidence}
    SR-->>RT: INTENT=CODE, conf=0.92

    RT->>TI: Issue JWT-ACE for CODE intent
    TI-->>RT: Token (tools: file_ops, terminal)

    RT->>LF: Start trace

    RT->>Sol: Solver: generate response
    Sol-->>RT: Initial code output

    RT->>Ver: Verify (AST + coherence + safety)
    Ver-->>RT: Score=0.85 → PASS

    RT->>LF: Log score, close trace

    RT-->>GW: Stream response (SSE)
    GW-->>UI: Forward stream
    UI-->>User: Display response

docs.title: 1. Request Ingress
docs.text: The Hive UI sends a POST /swarm/v1/chat/completions request to the Traefik reverse proxy on the Gateway node. Traefik routes requests matching /swarm/* to the Agent Runtime on the Execution node.

docs.title: 2. Intent Classification
docs.text:
The Semantic Router uses nemotron-orchestrator:8b to classify the user's message into one of 14 intent categories. The classification includes:

  • Intent: The category (CODE, IMAGE, CONVERSATION, etc.)
  • Confidence: 0.0–1.0 score
  • Reasoning: Short explanation of why this intent was chosen

If confidence is below 0.60 or the intent is AMBIGUOUS, the router retries with stronger prompts or falls back to CONVERSATION.

docs.title: 3. Token Issuance
docs.text:
The Token Issuer generates a JWT-ACE ephemeral capability token scoped to the intent. The token encodes:

  • Allowed tools (e.g., file_ops, terminal for CODE intent)
  • Security level (L1–L7)
  • Expiration time
  • Session ID and owner ID

docs.title: 4. Agent Execution
docs.text:
Depending on the intent, the request is dispatched to the appropriate agent:

Intent Agent Pipeline CODE, CONVERSATION, DEVOPS, DATA MarsRL Loop Solver → Verifier → Corrector IMAGE Image Agent ComfyUI pipeline 3D, ACTION_FIGURE 3D Pipeline Concept art → mesh reconstruction COORDINATE Coordinator Decompose → Research → Synthesize → Implement IOT_CONTROL IoT Agent Home Assistant API calls VISION Vision Agent VLM (Moondream) image analysis

docs.title: 5. MarsRL Verification
docs.text:
For coding and conversation requests, the MarsRL loop runs:

  1. Solver generates the initial response using qwen3.6:27b
  2. Verifier checks: AST syntax → coherence → safety (llama-guard-3)
  3. If score < 0.60, the Corrector rewrites (up to 2 iterations)
  4. Each step is traced in Langfuse with process-reward scores

docs.title: 6. Response Streaming
docs.text:
The response streams back as Server-Sent Events through the same path:

Agent Runtime → Traefik → Hive UI

Event types: status (progress updates), thought (reasoning), response (content), tool_call (tool invocations), error.

docs.title: Event Bus (Dispatcher)
docs.text:
For async tasks (image generation, 3D rendering), the Dispatcher queues events into Redis-backed queues:

graph LR
    RT[Agent Runtime] -->|emit| D[Dispatcher]
    D -->|queue:default| W1[Chat Workers x5]
    D -->|queue:image| W2[Image Workers x2]
    D -->|queue:3d| W3[3D Worker x1]
    D -->|queue:action_figure| W4[AF Worker x1]

Queue concurrency limits prevent GPU memory exhaustion.
