docs.title: Router
docs.text: The Semantic Router is the entry point for all user requests. It classifies intent, issues capability tokens, and dispatches to the appropriate agent.

docs.title: Files
docs.text: File Purpose agents/semantic_router.py Intent classification (Nemotron LLM) agents/router.py Request handling, token issuance, dispatch agents/intent_capabilities.py Intent → capability mapping

docs.title: Intent Classification
docs.text:
Uses nemotron-orchestrator:8b to classify messages into 14 intents:

INTENTS = [
    "CONVERSATION", "CODE", "DEVOPS", "DATA",
    "IMAGE", "3D", "ACTION_FIGURE",
    "RESEARCH", "DOCUMENTATION", "TRAIN",
    "IOT_CONTROL", "IOT_DEV", "VISION", "COORDINATE",
]

docs.title: Classification Prompt
docs.text:
The router sends a structured prompt to the LLM with:

  • The user's message
  • List of available intents with descriptions
  • Instructions to return JSON: {"intent": "...", "confidence": 0.0-1.0, "reasoning": "..."}

docs.title: Fallback Logic
docs.text: Condition Action Confidence ≥ 0.60 Accept intent Confidence < 0.60 Retry with stronger prompt Intent = AMBIGUOUS Retry or ask disambiguation question Timeout / error Fallback to CONVERSATION

docs.title: Token Issuance
docs.text:
After classification, the router generates a JWT-ACE token:

token = issue_token(
    intent="CODE",
    tools=["file_ops", "terminal", "ast_tool"],
    level="L4",
    session_id=session_id,
    owner_id=owner_id,
)

docs.title: Dispatch Table
docs.text: Intent Pipeline CODE, CONVERSATION, DEVOPS, DATA, DOCUMENTATION MarsRL Loop IMAGE Image Agent 3D, ACTION_FIGURE 3D Pipeline COORDINATE, RESEARCH Coordinator IOT_CONTROL IoT Agent IOT_DEV IoT Dev Agent TRAIN Memory System VISION Vision Agent

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Setting Value Description Router model nemotron-orchestrator:8b Intent classification LLM Min confidence 0.60 Below this triggers retry Max retries 2 Classification retry limit Timeout 10s LLM response timeout

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture: Agent System — design overview
  • Architecture: Data Flow — request lifecycle

docs.title: Training Dispatcher
docs.text: The Training Dispatcher is a FastAPI service running on Lovelace (192.168.2.101:8001). It accepts GRPO training job requests from Turing's agent_runtime, runs preflight checks, and manages grpo_trainer.py subprocesses using Lovelace's dual RTX 5060 Ti (32 GB VRAM).

docs.title: Files
docs.text: File Purpose agents/training/dispatcher.py FastAPI application — authentication, job queue, subprocess management agents/training/dataset_curator.py HuggingFace dataset downloader, security scanner, GRPO formatter agents/training/export_traces.py Langfuse trace exporter → GRPO JSONL agents/config.py ARCHETYPE_TRAINING_CONFIGS — per-archetype dataset and epoch settings config/source_whitelist.json Approved HuggingFace org slugs

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
graph LR
    AR["agent_runtime<br/>(Turing 192.168.2.103)"] -->|"POST /train<br/>X-Dispatcher-Key"| D["Training Dispatcher<br/>(Lovelace :8001)"]
    D --> PF[Preflight check]
    PF --> SP["grpo_trainer.py subprocess<br/>(RTX 5060 Ti × 2)"]
    SP --> O[Training output / adapter]

    DC["dataset_curator.py"] -->|curated JSONL| SP
    ET["export_traces.py"] -->|high-reward traces| DC
    LF["Langfuse<br/>(Hopper :3000)"] -->|scored traces| ET

Data flow:

  1. Langfuse stores agent traces scored by MarsRL.
  2. export_traces.py filters traces above the reward threshold (0.85) and exports them as GRPO JSONL.
  3. dataset_curator.py downloads additional HuggingFace datasets, applies security scanning, and converts to GRPO format.
  4. dispatcher.py receives a training job, validates the archetype, launches grpo_trainer.py.
  5. Caller polls GET /train/{job_id} until status is completed or failed.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All endpoints except GET /health require:

X-Dispatcher-Key: <DISPATCHER_SECRET>

DISPATCHER_SECRET is set in execution_plane/.env (Lovelace) and network.env (shared, so Turing's agent_runtime picks it up).

If DISPATCHER_SECRET is unset, all authenticated endpoints return 503 — the service is in locked mode.

docs.title: Endpoints
docs.text:
Method Path Auth Description GET /health None Liveness — returns status + available archetypes POST /train Required Submit a training job GET /train/{job_id} Required Poll job status GET /jobs Required List last 50 jobs (newest first) DELETE /train/{job_id} Required Cancel a running job

See the Training API Reference for full request/response schemas.

docs.title: Archetype Configs
docs.text:
Archetypes are defined in ARCHETYPE_TRAINING_CONFIGS in agents/config.py:

Archetype Datasets Epochs Use Case coder glaive-code-assistant, code-feedback 3 Code generation and review coordinator hermes-function-calling, slim-orca 2 Tool use and task delegation researcher openhermes, slim-orca 2 Multi-step reasoning creative openhermes 2 Creative writing and ideation

Submitting an unknown archetype returns 422 Unprocessable Entity.

docs.title: Curated Datasets
docs.text: Key HuggingFace ID Default Samples Format glaive-function-calling glaiveai/glaive-function-calling-v2 5,000 conversations hermes-function-calling NousResearch/hermes-function-calling-v1 5,000 conversations openhermes teknium/OpenHermes-2.5 10,000 conversations glaive-code-assistant glaiveai/glaive-code-assistant-v3 5,000 conversations slim-orca Open-Orca/SlimOrca 10,000 conversations code-feedback m-a-p/CodeFeedback-Filtered-Instruction 10,000 conversations the-stack-v2 bigcode/the-stack-v2-train-smol-ids 5,000 code

docs.title: Security Pipeline
docs.text:
Each sample passes two gates before being written to the output JSONL:

  1. Source whitelist (config/source_whitelist.json) — dataset org must be in 18 approved HuggingFace orgs. Missing whitelist file → fail-open (dev mode).
  2. llama-guard pre-scan — first 20 tokens sent to llama-guard-3:8b on Turing. Non-safe response → sample written to _rejected.jsonl. Network error → fail-open.

Fail-open policy

Both security gates are fail-open. This keeps local development unblocked when Turing or the whitelist file is unavailable. Strict mode is a future enhancement.

docs.title: Code Format Converter
docs.text:
Datasets with "format": "code" (e.g., the-stack-v2) use _code_to_grpo(), which wraps raw source files in a single instruction → completion turn:

{"prompt": "Provide the following code file:\n\n", "completion": "<source code>"}

docs.title: Trace Exporter
docs.text: export_traces.py queries Langfuse for high-reward traces and converts them to GRPO JSONL:

docs.title: Reward Threshold
docs.text:
Default: 0.85 (configurable via EXPORT_MIN_SCORE env var).

# Example: raise threshold for stricter quality
EXPORT_MIN_SCORE=0.90 python -m training.export_traces

docs.title: Deduplication
docs.text:
• ID dedup: tracks exported trace IDs in exported_ids.json (persists across runs).
  • Content dedup: skips samples whose output fingerprint (first 100 chars) was already seen in the current run.

docs.title: Topic Diversity
docs.text:
When total_limit is set, exports are capped per topic bucket to avoid imbalanced datasets:

Topic Keywords code function, class, import, def, ... math equation, calculate, proof, ... tool_use call, invoke, function, tool, ... creative story, poem, write, describe, ... general (everything else)

Cap per topic = total_limit ÷ number_of_topics.

docs.title: Docker Service
docs.text:
Defined in execution_plane/docker-compose.yml as training-dispatcher:

training-dispatcher:
  image: home-ai-lab/agent-runtime:latest
  container_name: training_dispatcher
  ports:
    - "8001:8001"
  env_file:
    - ../network.env
  environment:
    - DISPATCHER_SECRET=${DISPATCHER_SECRET}
  deploy:
    resources:
      reservations:
        devices:
          - driver: nvidia
            count: all
            capabilities: [gpu]

docs.title: Starting / Stopping
docs.text:
# Start
cd execution_plane
docker compose up -d training-dispatcher

# Check logs
docker logs -f training_dispatcher

# Stop
docker compose stop training-dispatcher

docs.title: Job State
docs.text:
In-memory state

Job history is stored in-memory only. A container restart clears all job records. A future phase will persist state to Redis or SQLite.

Status Meaning running Subprocess is active completed Subprocess exited with code 0 failed Subprocess exited with non-zero code cancelled DELETE /train/{job_id} was called

docs.title: BMO Agent
docs.text:
BMO is a character-driven voice assistant inspired by Adventure Time's BMO:

  • Personality: Cheerful, curious, slightly quirky
  • Voice: Custom voice model via RVC
  • Capabilities: All standard chat + voice output
  • Hardware: Can run on satellite devices (ESP32-S3)

docs.text:
1. Agent generates text response
  2. Text sent to Qwen3-TTS for speech synthesis
  3. Optionally processed through RVC for voice conversion
  4. Audio returned as WAV/MP3

docs.title: Satellite Devices
docs.text:
BMO can run on IoT satellite devices:

Device Purpose ESP32-S3 Wake word detection, audio capture Raspberry Pi Local voice processing

The satellite captures audio → sends to Agent Runtime → gets TTS response.

docs.title: ComfyUI
docs.text: Node-based image generation engine.

docs.title: Deployment

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: ComfyUI runs image generation workflows as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) of processing nodes. Memex submits workflows via the ComfyUI API.

docs.title: API Usage
docs.text:
import requests

# Queue a workflow
response = requests.post(
    f"http://192.168.2.101:8188/prompt",
    json={"prompt": workflow_json, "client_id": "agent-swarm"},
)
prompt_id = response.json()["prompt_id"]

# Check status
status = requests.get(f"http://192.168.2.101:8188/history/{prompt_id}")

docs.text: The web-based frontend for Memex.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
| Property | Value |

|----------|-------|

| URL | http://192.168.2.103/ |

| Framework | Web application |

| Directory | ui/ |

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Chat interface with streaming responses

  • Image gallery for generated artwork

  • 3D model viewer (Three.js)

  • Palace Viewer (/palace) with first-person room and drawer navigation

  • Session management

  • Status indicators (model loading, generation progress)

  • Dark/light theme

docs.title: Access
docs.text:
Open a browser and navigate to:

http://192.168.2.103/

Or via Tailscale for remote access.

docs.title: API Communication
docs.text:
The Hive UI uses a server-side proxy at /api/backend/* with split upstream routing:

| Upstream | Environment Variable | Used For |

|----------|----------------------|----------|

| Agent Runtime | API_BASE_URL | Identity, chat, training, ops, and general swarm APIs |

| MemPalace | MEMPALACE_BASE_URL | Palace Viewer layout, room, memory search, and memory CRUD |

Examples:

POST /swarm/v1/chat/completions

GET /api/backend/api/v1/identity

GET /api/backend/v1/palace/layout

Streaming chat continues to use Server-Sent Events through the Agent Runtime path.

For deployed gateway environments, the hive-ui container must define both API_BASE_URL and MEMPALACE_BASE_URL. Without the second variable, the Palace Viewer route renders but cannot load live Palace data.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• User Guide: Chat  using the chat interface

  • Getting Started: User Quickstart  first use

docs.title: Langfuse
docs.text: LLM observability and tracing service.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
| Property | Value |

|----------|-------|

| Node | Control Plane (192.168.2.102) |

| Port | 3000 |

| URL | http://192.168.2.102:3000 |

| Backend | ClickHouse + PostgreSQL |

| Compose | control_plane/docker-compose.yml |

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Langfuse captures end-to-end traces for every LLM interaction in Memex:

  • Traces: One per user request

  • Spans: Solver, Verifier, Corrector steps

  • Scores: Process-reward scores (0.01.0)

  • Generations: Individual LLM calls with tokens/latency

  • Metadata: Intent, model, template version

docs.title: Integration
docs.text:
The Agent Runtime uses the Langfuse Python SDK:

from langfuse import Langfuse



lf = Langfuse(

    public_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY"],

    secret_key=os.environ["LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"],

    host=os.environ["LANGFUSE_HOST"],

)



trace = lf.trace(name="chat_request", session_id=session_id)

span = trace.span(name="solver")

# ... generation ...

span.end()

trace.score(name="verification", value=0.85)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
| Variable | Description |

|----------|-------------|

| LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY | API public key |

| LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY | API secret key |

| LANGFUSE_HOST | Service URL |

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Architecture: Observability

  • Admin: Monitoring

docs.title: MemPalace
docs.text:
Hierarchical semantic memory service. Provides vector-searchable memories, versioned agent snapshots, and shared team scratchpads behind a single FastAPI service. Backed by PostgreSQL/pgvector and Ollama embeddings.

Operator-facing reference. For architecture and design rationale, see Architecture Deep Dive. For the full HTTP API, see Developer Guide → MemPalace API.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: Property Value Node Control Plane (192.168.2.102) Container mempalace Port 8200 URL http://192.168.2.102:8200 MCP http://192.168.2.102:8200/mcp Backend PostgreSQL (mempalace schema) with pgvector Image control_plane-mempalace Compose file control_plane/docker-compose.yml Image source control_plane/mempalace/Dockerfile Runtime user non-root (UID 1000) Healthcheck Docker HEALTHCHECK against /health

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Environment variable Default Purpose MEMPALACE_DB_USER langfuse (via LANGFUSE_DB_USER) Postgres user MEMPALACE_DB_PASSWORD (required, via LANGFUSE_DB_PASSWORD) Postgres password MEMPALACE_DB_HOST postgres (compose service name) Postgres host MEMPALACE_DB_PORT 5432 Postgres port MEMPALACE_DB_NAME langfuse (via LANGFUSE_DB_NAME) Database name (mempalace lives in its own schema) OLLAMA_HOST http://localhost:11434 (overridden in compose to http://192.168.2.101:11434) Ollama endpoint EMBED_MODEL nomic-embed-text Embedding model EXTRACT_MODEL qwen2.5-coder:14b-instruct-q4_k_m LLM used for memory extraction OLLAMA_TIMEOUT 60 Per-request HTTP timeout (seconds) OLLAMA_EMBED_RETRIES 2 Retry count on transient embed failures LOG_LEVEL INFO Standard Python logging level

The Postgres credentials are deliberately shared with the Langfuse database; MemPalace uses a separate schema (mempalace.*) inside the same DB so a single Postgres instance serves both control-plane services.

docs.title: Schema
docs.text:
Five application tables plus the Alembic version tracker, all under the mempalace schema:

Table Purpose mempalace.memories Semantic memories (the dominant table) mempalace.agent_snapshots Versioned per-agent learned state mempalace.team_memories Coordinator team scratchpad mempalace.memory_audit_log Append-only audit trail mempalace.extraction_log Per-attempt extraction audit mempalace.alembic_version Single-row migration tracker

Schema is managed by Alembic — the app runs alembic upgrade head on boot. Adding a new column or constraint is a 2-step procedure documented in Procedures → MemPalace Migrations.

docs.title: Health & status
docs.text:
# Liveness
curl -fsS http://192.168.2.102:8200/health
# {"status":"ok","service":"mempalace"}

# Schema state
docker compose -f control_plane/docker-compose.yml exec mempalace alembic current
# 0002_add_unique_constraints (head)

# Counts by type/domain
curl -fsS http://192.168.2.102:8200/v1/memories/stats | jq

# Per-owner extraction success rate
curl -fsS http://192.168.2.102:8200/v1/palace/audit/extractions | jq

docs.title: API surface (overview)
docs.text:
15 REST endpoints under /v1/... and 4 MCP tools at /mcp. Quick reference:

Method Path Purpose GET /health Liveness POST /v1/memories Store a memory (writes audit created) POST /v1/memories/search Semantic search PATCH /v1/memories/{id} Update + audit DELETE /v1/memories/{id} Delete + audit GET /v1/memories/stats Counts by type/domain GET /v1/memories/{id}/audit Audit trail POST /v1/extract Run extractor + store + log (single tx) POST / GET /v1/snapshots Versioned agent state (race-safe) POST / GET / DELETE /v1/team/{team_id} Team-scoped key/value POST /v1/team/{team_id}/search Team semantic search GET /v1/palace/layout 3D viewer hierarchy GET /v1/palace/room Memories at a palace coordinate GET /v1/palace/audit/extractions Extraction audit

Full request/response contracts: Developer Guide → MemPalace API.

docs.title: Restart
docs.text:
ssh misterobots@192.168.2.102
cd ~/Agent_Swarm/control_plane
docker compose restart mempalace
docker compose logs --tail=50 mempalace

docs.title: Roll a new image
docs.text:
cd ~/Agent_Swarm
git pull
cd control_plane
docker compose build mempalace
docker compose up -d --force-recreate mempalace

If the new image introduces an Alembic migration, the app will apply it on boot. For first-time Alembic adoption on an existing DB (one-shot only), see the migrations procedure.

docs.title: Backup the schema
docs.text:
docker exec postgres pg_dump -U langfuse --schema=mempalace langfuse \
  > ~/mempalace_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).sql

docs.title: Restore
docs.text:
docker exec -i postgres psql -U langfuse -d langfuse \
  < ~/mempalace_backup_<timestamp>.sql

docs.title: Query the live DB
docs.text:
docker exec -it postgres psql -U langfuse -d langfuse
# \dt mempalace.*
# SELECT memory_type, domain, count(*) FROM mempalace.memories GROUP BY 1, 2;

docs.title: Failure modes
docs.text: Symptom Likely cause First check Boot fails with Alembic error Live DB hasn't been stamped at baseline alembic current — if blank, see migrations procedure POST /v1/memories returns 500 Ollama unreachable for embedding curl http://192.168.2.101:11434/api/tags Extraction returns [] consistently LLM model not pulled or returning malformed JSON Check extraction_log + mempalace container logs 4xx with owner_id is required Caller did not include owner_id in body All writes require owner_id; same constraint applies to MCP tools Slow /v1/memories/search IVFFlat index degenerate (built on empty table) REINDEX INDEX mempalace.ix_mem_embedding; once data volume is meaningful

OpenAI-compatible API:

# Chat completion

curl http://192.168.2.101:11434/v1/chat/completions \

    -d '{"model": "qwen3.6:27b", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]}'



# List models

curl http://192.168.2.101:11434/api/tags



# Pull model

curl -X POST http://192.168.2.101:11434/api/pull \

    -d '{"name": "qwen3.6:27b"}'

docs.title: OpenHands
docs.text: Sandboxed code execution environment.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: Property Value Node Execution (192.168.2.101) Port 3300 Isolation Docker-in-Docker Compose execution_plane/docker-compose.yml

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: OpenHands provides a sandboxed environment for executing user code safely. Code runs in an isolated Docker container that cannot affect the host system.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Full Python / Node.js / shell environment
  • File system isolation
  • Network restrictions
  • Resource limits (CPU, memory, time)
  • Output capture and streaming

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• Docker-in-Docker isolation
  • No access to host filesystem
  • Limited network access
  • Execution timeouts
  • Resource quotas

  • Architecture: Security Model

docs.title: SPIRE
docs.text: Workload identity service (SPIFFE Runtime Environment).

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: Component Node Port SPIRE Server Control (192.168.2.102) 8081 SPIRE Agent Execution (192.168.2.101) Unix socket SPIRE Agent Gateway (192.168.2.103) Unix socket

docs.title: Trust Domain
docs.text: spiffe://home-ai-lab

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
SPIRE provides cryptographic workload identity:

  • X.509 SVIDs: Short-lived certificates for mutual TLS
  • Join tokens: One-time attestation tokens for agent bootstrap
  • Workload attestation: Verify workload identity via Unix selectors

docs.title: Key Commands
docs.text:
# Server health
spire-server healthcheck

# Generate join token
spire-server token generate -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/node -ttl 3600

# List entries
spire-server entry show

# Agent health
spire-agent healthcheck

Dangerous patterns are intercepted:

  • curl | bash, wget | sh — arbitrary code download
  • chmod 777 — permission weakening

docs.title: Procedures
docs.text:
Step-by-step operational runbooks for common administrative tasks.

Procedure Description Add a New Model Pull and configure a new Ollama model Switch Models Change the solver, router, or verifier model Rotate SPIRE Keys Regenerate SPIRE join tokens and certificates Configure Alerting Set up jacquard alerts and AlertManager Onboard a User Set up a new user with access Disaster Recovery Full system recovery from backup GPU Troubleshooting Diagnose and fix GPU issues Database Maintenance PostgreSQL vacuum, backup, restore MemPalace Migrations Add an Alembic migration, baseline-stamp a live DB, deploy schema changes Log Analysis Query and analyze logs in knuth Performance Tuning Optimize inference speed and throughput

docs.title: Add a New Model
docs.text: Pull a new model from the Ollama registry and make it available.

docs.title: 1. Check Available VRAM
docs.text:
docker exec ollama nvidia-smi

Verify there's enough VRAM for the new model.

docs.title: 2. Pull the Model
docs.text:
docker exec ollama ollama pull <model-name>:<tag>

Example:

docker exec ollama ollama pull gemma3:12b

docs.title: 3. Test the Model
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://192.168.2.101:11434/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "model": "gemma3:12b",
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello, how are you?"}]
    }'

docs.title: 4. (Optional) Create a Modelfile
docs.text:
For custom system prompts or parameters:

cat > /tmp/Modelfile << 'EOF'
FROM gemma3:12b
PARAMETER temperature 0.7
SYSTEM "You are a helpful assistant."
EOF

docker cp /tmp/Modelfile ollama:/tmp/Modelfile
docker exec ollama ollama create gemma3-custom -f /tmp/Modelfile

docs.title: 5. Verify
docs.text:
curl http://192.168.2.101:11434/api/tags | python -m json.tool

The model should appear in the list.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Model downloads can be large (2–20 GB). Ensure sufficient disk space.
  • The Gateway's secondary Ollama (port 11435) can also host models.

docs.title: Configure Alerting
docs.text: Set up jacquard alerts and AlertManager notifications.

docs.title: 1. Define Alert Rules
docs.text:
Edit turing_gateway/config/jacquard/alert_rules.yml:

groups:
  - name: agent-swarm
    rules:
      - alert: HighErrorRate
        expr: rate(http_requests_total{status=~"5.."}[5m]) > 0.05
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "High error rate detected"

      - alert: ForgeDown
        expr: up{job="forge"} == 0
        for: 2m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Ollama is unreachable"

docs.title: 2. Configure AlertManager
docs.text:
Edit turing_gateway/config/alertmanager/alertmanager.yml:

global:
  smtp_smarthost: 'smtp.gmail.com:587'
  smtp_from: 'alerts@example.com'

route:
  receiver: 'default'
  routes:
    - match:
        severity: critical
      receiver: 'email'

receivers:
  - name: 'default'
    webhook_configs:
      - url: 'http://ntfy-server/alerts'
  - name: 'email'
    email_configs:
      - to: 'admin@example.com'

docs.title: 3. Restart Services
docs.text: docker compose restart jacquard alertmanager

docs.title: 4. Test Alerts
docs.text:
# Manually fire a test alert
curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103:9093/api/v1/alerts \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '[{"labels":{"alertname":"TestAlert","severity":"warning"},"annotations":{"summary":"Test alert"}}]'

Check that the notification arrives.

docs.title: Database Maintenance
docs.text: PostgreSQL maintenance tasks: vacuum, indexing, backup verification.

docs.title: Routine Vacuum
docs.text:
# Vacuum analyze all tables
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c "VACUUM ANALYZE;"

docs.title: Check Database Size
docs.text:
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c \
    "SELECT pg_size_pretty(pg_database_size('agent_swarm'));"

docs.title: Verify Backup Integrity
docs.text:
# Test restore to a temporary database
docker compose exec postgres createdb -U postgres test_restore
docker compose exec -T postgres pg_restore -U postgres -d test_restore < backup.dump
docker compose exec postgres dropdb -U postgres test_restore

docs.title: pgvector Maintenance
docs.text:
# Reindex vector indexes
docker compose exec postgres psql -U postgres -c \
    "REINDEX INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_embeddings_vector;"

docs.title: Scheduled Maintenance
docs.text:
Add to crontab:

# Weekly vacuum
0 4 * * 0 docker compose -f /opt/Agent_Swarm/control_plane/docker-compose.yml exec -T postgres psql -U postgres -c "VACUUM ANALYZE;"

docs.title: Disaster Recovery
docs.text: Full system recovery from backup.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Access to backup files (see Backup & Restore)
  • Fresh node deployments ready

docs.title: 1. Deploy Fresh Nodes
docs.text:
Follow the deployment guides in order:

  1. Control Plane
  2. Execution Plane
  3. Gateway

docs.title: 2. Restore PostgreSQL
docs.text:
# On Control Node
docker compose exec -T postgres pg_restore \
    -U postgres -d postgres --clean \
    < /backups/postgres.dump

docs.title: 3. Restore Workspace
docs.text:
# On Execution Node
docker cp workspace.tar.gz agent-runtime:/tmp/
docker compose exec agent-runtime tar xzf /tmp/workspace.tar.gz -C /

docs.title: 4. Re-Pull Models
docs.text:
docker exec ollama ollama pull qwen3.6:27b
docker exec ollama ollama pull nemotron-orchestrator:8b
docker exec ollama ollama pull llama-guard-3:8b

docs.title: 5. Re-Attest SPIRE Agents
docs.text: Generate fresh join tokens and attest both agents. See Rotate SPIRE Keys.

docs.title: 6. Verify
docs.text: Run the full Post-Deployment Verification.

docs.title: Recovery Time Estimate
docs.text: Component Time Deploy nodes 30 min Restore databases 10 min Pull models 20 min SPIRE attestation 5 min Verification 10 min Total ~75 min

docs.title: GPU Troubleshooting
docs.text: Diagnose and resolve GPU-related issues.

docs.title: Quick Diagnostic
docs.text:
# Check GPU visibility
nvidia-smi

# Check Docker GPU access
docker run --rm --gpus all nvidia/cuda:12.4.1-base-ubuntu22.04 nvidia-smi

# Check Ollama GPU usage
docker exec ollama nvidia-smi

docs.title: GPU Not Visible in Container
docs.text:
Symptom: nvidia-smi works on host but not in container.

Fix:

# Install nvidia-container-toolkit
sudo apt install nvidia-container-toolkit
sudo systemctl restart docker

docs.title: OOM (Out of Memory)
docs.text:
Symptom: Model inference crashes with CUDA OOM error.

Fix:

  1. Reduce loaded models: 

    # In docker-compose.yml
OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS=2

  2. Use smaller quantization: 

    docker exec ollama ollama pull qwen3.6:27b:q4_K_M

  3. Restart Ollama to clear VRAM: 

    docker compose restart ollama

docs.title: Slow Inference
docs.text:
Symptom: Token generation much slower than expected.

Check:

  1. Verify Flash Attention: OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1
  2. Check VRAM pressure: nvidia-smi — VRAM near 100%?
  3. Check thermal throttling: nvidia-smi -q -d TEMPERATURE
  4. Ensure no background GPU tasks: close other GPU apps

docs.title: ComfyUI VRAM Conflict
docs.text:
Symptom: Image generation fails while Ollama models are loaded.

Fix: ComfyUI and Ollama share the GPU. When ComfyUI needs VRAM:

  1. Ollama will auto-unload idle models
  2. Reduce OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=5m for faster unloading
  3. Or use queue limits in the Dispatcher to prevent concurrent access

docs.title: Log Analysis
docs.text: Query and analyze logs using knuth and hollerith.

docs.title: Accessing Logs
docs.text: Open hollerith at http://192.168.2.103:3001, select the knuth data source, and use the Explore tab.

docs.title: All Errors
docs.text: {container_name="agent-runtime"} |= "ERROR"

docs.title: Intent Classification
docs.text: {container_name="agent-runtime"} |= "Intent classified"

docs.title: MarsRL Failures
docs.text: {container_name="agent-runtime"} |= "Verifier" |= "FAIL"

docs.title: Ollama Errors
docs.text: {container_name=~"ollama.*"} | json | level="error"

docs.title: Specific Session
docs.text: {container_name="agent-runtime"} |= "session=abc123"

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Use time range filters to narrow results
  • Pipe through | json for structured log parsing
  • Use | line_format for custom output formatting
  • Export results for offline analysis

docs.title: MemPalace Migrations
docs.text:
End-to-end procedures for evolving the MemPalace database schema. Schema is managed by Alembic — the runtime app calls alembic upgrade head on boot, so deployed code refuses to start unless the DB is migrated to a compatible state.

Source files: control_plane/mempalace/alembic/

docs.title: Where the schema lives
docs.text:
Artifact Purpose control_plane/mempalace/alembic.ini Alembic config (DB URL is blank — overridden in env.py from env vars) control_plane/mempalace/alembic/env.py Async migration runner; restricts to the mempalace schema; imports models from app.database control_plane/mempalace/alembic/versions/*.py One file per migration control_plane/mempalace/alembic/script.py.mako Template for new migrations mempalace.alembic_version (DB) Single-row table tracking current revision

The ORM models in app/database.py are the source of truth at the Python level — when models and migrations disagree, autogenerate tells you. Migrations are the source of truth at the database level — they're what actually runs against Postgres.

docs.title: Procedure A — Add a new migration
docs.text: The everyday workflow when you need to add a column, index, or constraint.

docs.title: 1. Edit the model
docs.text:
Make the change in control_plane/mempalace/app/database.py. Example: adding a tags column to Memory:

class Memory(Base):
    __tablename__ = "memories"
    # ...
    tags = Column(JSONB, server_default=text("'[]'::jsonb"))

docs.title: 2. Generate the migration
docs.text:
From inside the running container (or any environment with the deps):

docker compose -f control_plane/docker-compose.yml \
  exec mempalace alembic revision --autogenerate -m "add tags column to memories"

This produces control_plane/mempalace/alembic/versions/000N_add_tags_column_to_memories.py.

docs.title: 3. Review the generated migration
docs.text:
Always read the autogenerated file before committing. Common pitfalls:

  • pgvector Vector(768) columns sometimes render as sa.NullType() — edit the file to use from pgvector.sqlalchemy import Vector and Vector(768).
  • server_default values may need adjustment (autogenerate guesses based on the model).
  • IVFFlat / HNSW index options aren't always preserved — copy from the baseline migration if you're touching vector indexes.
# Example of what to review:
def upgrade() -> None:
    op.add_column(
        "memories",
        sa.Column("tags", JSONB(), server_default=sa.text("'[]'::jsonb")),
        schema="mempalace",
    )

def downgrade() -> None:
    op.drop_column("memories", "tags", schema="mempalace")

docs.title: 4. Test on a dev DB
docs.text:
Bring up a fresh local instance, let it run all migrations from baseline, then verify schema matches expectations:

docker compose -f control_plane/docker-compose.yml down -v mempalace postgres
docker compose -f control_plane/docker-compose.yml up -d
docker compose exec postgres psql -U langfuse -d langfuse -c "\d mempalace.memories"

docs.title: 5. Commit and deploy
docs.text: Commit the model change and the generated migration as one unit. Deploy following Procedure C.

docs.title: Procedure B — Adopt Alembic on an existing DB
docs.text: One-time procedure for environments that have tables created by the pre-Alembic Base.metadata.create_all and do not yet have an alembic_version row. Skipping the stamp will cause boot to fail with "relation already exists" when 0001_baseline tries to recreate tables that already exist.

docs.title: Steps (run on the host where mempalace deploys)
docs.text:
ssh misterobots@192.168.2.102
cd ~/Agent_Swarm/control_plane

# 1. Pull the new code (which has alembic baked in)
cd ~/Agent_Swarm && git pull
cd control_plane

# 2. Build the new image (boot will require alembic in PATH)
docker compose build mempalace

# 3. STAMP first — tells alembic the DB is already at baseline state
#    Use `run --rm` so we don't start the long-lived container yet:
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint alembic mempalace stamp 0001_baseline

# 4. Verify the stamp landed
docker compose run --rm --entrypoint alembic mempalace current
# Expected: 0001_baseline

# 5. Now safe to bring up the new container — boot runs `upgrade head`
#    which applies any later migrations (e.g. 0002_add_unique_constraints)
docker compose up -d --force-recreate mempalace

# 6. Confirm
docker compose logs --tail=20 mempalace
docker compose exec mempalace alembic current   # should show the head revision
curl -fsS http://192.168.2.102:8200/health

This procedure was used to introduce Alembic to the live mempalace DB on 2026-05-08. Run it once per environment.

docs.title: Pre-deploy backup (recommended)
docs.text:
docker exec postgres pg_dump -U langfuse --schema=mempalace langfuse \
  > ~/mempalace_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).sql

The dump is ~1.6 MB per ~150 memories. Keep until the new state is confirmed stable.

docs.title: Procedure C — Deploy a migration
docs.text:
Standard deploy flow once Alembic is already adopted on the target DB.

ssh misterobots@192.168.2.102
cd ~/Agent_Swarm
git pull
cd control_plane
docker compose build mempalace
docker compose up -d --force-recreate mempalace
docker compose logs --tail=30 mempalace

The boot sequence runs init_db() which:

  1. CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector (idempotent)
  2. CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS mempalace (idempotent)
  3. alembic upgrade head (applies any pending migrations)

If migrations fail, the FastAPI app does not start and the container will fail its HEALTHCHECK.

docs.title: Procedure D — Roll back a migration
docs.text: Use sparingly. Migrations are the historical record — preferring "fix forward" with a new migration is usually safer.

docs.title: Roll back one revision
docs.text:
docker compose -f control_plane/docker-compose.yml \
  exec mempalace alembic downgrade -1

The container is still running on the old code, which now mismatches the DB. Roll the container back to the matching code before the next request:

git checkout <previous-commit>
docker compose build mempalace
docker compose up -d --force-recreate mempalace

docs.title: Roll back to a specific revision
docs.text: docker compose exec mempalace alembic downgrade 0001_baseline

docs.title: Restore from backup (last resort)
docs.text:
docker exec -i postgres psql -U langfuse -d langfuse \
  < ~/mempalace_backup_<timestamp>.sql

docs.title: Migration history
docs.text:
Revision Title Notes 0001_baseline baseline schema 5 tables, 8 indexes (incl. ivfflat) 0002_add_unique_constraints snapshot + team uniqueness Defensive dedupe + UNIQUE constraints (closes the SELECT-then-INSERT race)

Apply the live current revision check anytime:

docker compose -f control_plane/docker-compose.yml \
  exec mempalace alembic current

docs.title: Common scenarios
docs.text: Scenario Procedure Adding a column / index / constraint A (add migration) → C (deploy) Adopting Alembic on a live DB for the first time B (one-time stamp) Routine deploy (no schema changes) C as usual — upgrade head is a no-op Bringing up a fresh dev environment Just docker compose up; baseline runs from scratch Migration broke prod, need to revert D (downgrade) + redeploy old image; investigate; fix forward

docs.title: "relation already exists" at boot
docs.text:
Cause: Live DB already has the tables (from pre-Alembic create_all) but no alembic_version row exists. Boot tries to run 0001_baseline which fails on CREATE TABLE memories.

Fix: Run Procedure B.

docs.title: "Target database is not up to date"
docs.text:
Cause: A schema change exists in the model but no migration was generated, or a migration was generated but not committed.

Fix: Run alembic revision --autogenerate -m "..." and review/commit the result.

docs.title: Autogenerate emits sa.NullType() for embedding columns
docs.text:
Cause: Alembic's autogenerator doesn't know about pgvector's Vector type by default.

Fix: Hand-edit the migration:

from pgvector.sqlalchemy import Vector

# Replace this:
sa.Column("embedding", sa.NullType())
# With:
sa.Column("embedding", Vector(768))

alembic/env.py already imports Vector so the runtime side is fine — only the autogen output needs editing.

docs.title: "Invalid value for sqlalchemy.url"
docs.text:
Cause: alembic.ini has sqlalchemy.url = (blank) and the env vars that env.py reads (MEMPALACE_DB_USER / _PASSWORD / _HOST / etc.) are not set in the calling environment.

Fix: Run alembic via docker compose exec mempalace ... so the container's env vars (set by compose) apply automatically.

docs.title: Live container fails HEALTHCHECK after deploy
docs.text:
Cause: Migration ran cleanly but the app code references a column or table that doesn't yet exist (rare — usually means the migration was incomplete).

Fix: docker compose logs mempalace, identify the missing schema element, write a follow-up migration, deploy again.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Service: MemPalace — operator reference
  • MemPalace Architecture Deep Dive § Schema Migrations
  • MemPalace API Reference
  • Database Maintenance — broader Postgres ops
  • Disaster Recovery — full-system restore flow

docs.title: Onboard a User
docs.text: Set up a new user with access to Memex.

docs.title: 1. Provide Access URL
docs.text:
Give the user the Hive UI URL:

  • LAN: http://192.168.2.103/
  • Remote: Share Tailscale URL

docs.title: 2. Create Owner ID
docs.text:
Assign a unique owner_id for the user. This is used to scope:

  • Session history
  • Skills Memory rules
  • Training data
  • Preferences

docs.title: 3. Orient the User
docs.text:
Share these documentation pages:

  1. Getting Started: User Quickstart
  2. User Guide: Chat
  3. User Guide: Settings

docs.title: 4. Set Initial Preferences
docs.text:
The user can teach preferences via chat:

"Remember that I prefer detailed code comments" "I like responses in bullet point format"

These are stored in Skills Memory under their owner_id.

docs.title: 5. Verify Access
docs.text: Have the user send a test message through the Hive UI to confirm end-to-end functionality.

docs.title: Performance Tuning
docs.text: Optimize inference speed and system throughput.

docs.title: Flash Attention
docs.text:
Ensure Flash Attention is enabled:

OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1

docs.title: Concurrent Requests
docs.text:
Balance parallelism with VRAM:

Setting Low VRAM (8 GB) Medium (16 GB) High (24 GB+) OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL 1 2 4 OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS 1 3 5

docs.title: Context Window
docs.text:
Smaller context windows reduce VRAM and improve speed:

# In Modelfile
PARAMETER num_ctx 4096   # instead of 32768

docs.title: Stream Timeout
docs.text:
Increase for large responses:

STREAM_TIMEOUT = 120.0  # seconds

docs.title: MarsRL Tuning
docs.text:
For faster responses at the cost of quality verification:

Setting Faster Safer max_iter 1 2 pass_threshold 0.50 0.70

docs.title: Docker Resource Limits
docs.text:
services:
  agent-runtime:
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 8G
          cpus: "4.0"

docs.title: Network Latency
docs.text:
• Verify LAN latency: ping 192.168.2.101 should be < 1ms
  • Use wired connections for GPU nodes (not WiFi)

docs.title: Benchmarking
docs.text:
# Simple latency test
time curl -s -X POST http://192.168.2.103/swarm/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}], "stream": false}'

Target: < 5 seconds for simple chat responses.

docs.title: Rotate SPIRE Keys
docs.text: Regenerate SPIRE join tokens and re-attest agents.

docs.title: When to Rotate
docs.text:
• Regularly (quarterly recommended)
  • After a security incident
  • When adding/removing nodes

docs.title: 1. Generate New Tokens
docs.text:
On the Control Node:

# Token for Execution Node
docker compose exec spire-server \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server token generate \
    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/execution-node \
    -ttl 3600

# Token for Gateway Node
docker compose exec spire-server \
    /opt/spire/bin/spire-server token generate \
    -spiffeID spiffe://home-ai-lab/Turing-gateway \
    -ttl 3600

Save both tokens.

docs.title: 2. Update Execution Node
docs.text:
# Stop SPIRE agent
docker compose stop spire-agent

# Update join token in agent.conf
nano execution_plane/config/spire/agent.conf
# Set: join_token = "<new-token>"

# Restart
docker compose up -d spire-agent

docs.title: 3. Update Gateway Node
docs.text:
# Same process on Turing
docker compose stop spire-agent
nano turing_gateway/config/spire/agent.conf
docker compose up -d spire-agent

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text:
# Check both agents
docker compose exec spire-agent /opt/spire/bin/spire-agent healthcheck

Both agents should report healthy.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Join tokens are single-use — each agent needs its own token
  • Tokens expire after TTL — use them promptly
  • Existing SVIDs remain valid until their TTL expires

docs.title: Switch Models
docs.text: Change the solver, router, or verifier model used by Memex.

docs.title: 1. Pull the New Model
docs.text: docker exec ollama ollama pull <new-model>

docs.title: 2. Update Environment
docs.text:
Edit network.env:

# Change from:
SOLVER_MODEL=qwen3.6:27b
# To:
SOLVER_MODEL=qwen3.5:14b

docs.title: 3. Restart Agent Runtime
docs.text:
cd execution_plane
docker compose restart agent-runtime

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://192.168.2.103/swarm/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What model are you using?"}],
        "stream": false
    }'

Check the response metadata for the new model name.

docs.title: 5. Monitor
docs.text:
Watch Langfuse traces and hollerith dashboards for:

  • Response quality changes
  • Latency differences
  • Error rate changes

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
If the new model performs poorly:

  1. Revert network.env to the previous model
  2. Restart Agent Runtime: docker compose restart agent-runtime
  3. The previous model should still be cached in Ollama

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
Supplementary reference material for Memex.

  • Glossary — definitions of terms and acronyms
  • Environment Variables — complete variable reference
  • Changelog — release history
  • Contributing — how to contribute
  • License — project license

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: All notable changes to Memex are documented here.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Comprehensive MkDocs documentation site

  • Docker-based documentation deployment

  • GitHub Pages deployment via GitHub Actions

docs.title: Changed
docs.text: • Documentation restructured as a full library of procedures, tutorials, and references

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Multi-agent orchestration (Router ? Coordinator ? Solver)

  • MarsRL verification loop (solve ? verify ? reward)

  • Image generation via ComfyUI integration

  • 3D model generation pipeline

  • Voice interface (Whisper STT + Piper TTS)

  • IoT control via Home Assistant

  • Research Mode with web search

  • Skills Memory (PostgreSQL + pgvector)

  • SPIRE-based service identity and mTLS

  • Langfuse observability and tracing

  • jacquard + hollerith monitoring

  • knuth log aggregation

  • Traefik reverse proxy and load balancing

  • GRPO preference training

  • Governance system for sensitive operations

  • Hive UI web interface

  • GPU allocation and management

  • 3-node distributed architecture

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text:
• Docker Compose deployment across 3 nodes

  • Control Plane: SPIRE server, PostgreSQL, Langfuse

  • Execution Plane: Ollama, Agent Runtime, ComfyUI, Voice

  • Gateway: Traefik, jacquard, hollerith, knuth

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to Memex.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Clone your fork
  3. Set up a local development environment
  4. Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feature/my-feature

docs.title: Development Workflow
docs.text:
1. Make your changes
  2. Write or update tests
  3. Run tests: pytest
  4. Commit with a clear message: git commit -m "Add: my new feature"
  5. Push to your fork: git push origin feature/my-feature
  6. Open a Pull Request

docs.title: Code Standards
docs.text:
• Python: follow PEP 8
  • Use type hints for function signatures
  • Add docstrings for public functions and classes
  • Log errors with contextual information

docs.title: Pull Request Guidelines
docs.text:
• One feature/fix per PR
  • Include a clear description of what and why
  • Reference related issues
  • Ensure all tests pass
  • Keep PRs focused and reasonably sized

docs.title: Areas for Contribution
docs.text:
• New Agents — see Adding Agents
  • New Tools — see Adding Tools
  • Documentation — fix typos, improve clarity, add examples
  • Bug Fixes — check open issues
  • Tests — improve coverage

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text:
Open a GitHub issue with:

  • Clear description of the problem
  • Steps to reproduce
  • Expected vs. actual behavior
  • Logs or error messages
  • Environment details (OS, GPU, Docker version)

docs.title: Documentation Contributions
docs.text:
Documentation lives in docs-site/docs/. To preview:

cd docs-site
pip install -r requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

Then open http://localhost:8000.

docs.title: Documentation Standards
docs.text:
Document ID: DOC-REF-0001
Domain: Reference
Owner: Core Platform
Status: Approved
Version: 1.0
Last Updated: 2026-04-16

Every page in the Memex DocSite must follow this standard template to ensure consistency, traceability, and completeness.

docs.title: Standard Template
docs.text: All documentation pages must include the following sections:

docs.title: 1. YAML Frontmatter
docs.text:
---
title: "Page Title"
doc_id: "DOC-CATEGORY-NNNN"
last_updated: "YYYY-MM-DD"
source_ref: "URL or project name"
---
Field Format Description title String Display title for the page doc_id DOC-{CAT}-{NNNN} Unique document identifier last_updated YYYY-MM-DD Last modification date source_ref URL or name Original source/project reference

docs.title: 2. Document ID Badge
docs.text:
Immediately after the H1 heading, include a metadata code block:

```
Document ID: DOC-MOD-0042
Domain: Module
Owner: Core Platform
Status: Active
Version: 1.0
Last Updated: 2026-04-16
```
Field Description Document ID Unique DOC-{CAT}-{NNNN} identifier Domain Category name (Architecture, Module, Security, etc.) Owner Responsible team Status Active, Approved, Draft, or Deprecated Version Semantic version of the document Last Updated ISO date of last modification

docs.title: 3. Source Citations & References
docs.text:
A table listing all external projects, libraries, and sources:

| # | Source | URL / Path | License | Notes |
|---|--------|------------|---------|-------|
| 1 | Ollama | https://ollama.ai | MIT | LLM inference engine |

docs.title: 4. Changelog (Source → Implementation)
docs.text:
Tracks how the original project was adapted for the Hive:

| Date | Change | Source Version | Hive Version | Author |
|------|--------|----------------|--------------|--------|
| 2026-01-15 | Initial integration | v0.1.0 | Phase 3 | Hive Team |

docs.title: 5. Overview
docs.text: A concise description of what the component does and why it exists.

docs.title: 6. Usage in the Hive
docs.text:
Three subsections:

  • When This Module Is Invoked — intents, triggers, conditions
  • How to Use — step-by-step from the UI or API
  • Configuration — environment variables and settings table

docs.title: 7. Maintenance & Updates
docs.text:
Three subsections:

  • How to Update — commands, Docker steps, procedures
  • Dependencies — upstream projects, internal deps
  • Health Checks — verification commands and endpoints

docs.title: 8. Functionality Testing
docs.text:
Two subsections:

  • Automated Tests — pytest files, CI commands
  • Manual Verification — step-by-step manual checks

docs.title: 9. UI, Screenshots & Examples
docs.text:
All images must use the lightbox format for expandable zoom:

<figure markdown="span">
  ![Description](path/to/image.png){ loading=lazy }
  <figcaption>Caption describing the screenshot</figcaption>
</figure>

Image Best Practices

  • Use PNG for screenshots, SVG for diagrams
  • Store images in docs/assets/ with descriptive names
  • Include { loading=lazy } on every image for glightbox zoom
  • Add a <figcaption> for accessibility

For placeholder screenshots:

!!! example "Screenshot Needed"
    <!-- TODO: Add screenshot of [specific element] -->

docs.title: 10. Related Documentation
docs.text:
Links to other relevant DocSite pages using relative paths:

- [Architecture: Topic](../architecture/topic.md)
- [Module: Name](../modules/name.md)

docs.title: Document ID Categories
docs.text: Category Prefix Used For Architecture DOC-ARCH-NNNN Architecture docs, deep dives Module DOC-MOD-NNNN Core and specialized module docs Procedure DOC-PROC-NNNN Operational procedures Tutorial DOC-TUT-NNNN Step-by-step tutorials Admin Guide DOC-ADM-NNNN Admin and deployment docs Developer Guide DOC-DEV-NNNN Developer reference User Guide DOC-USR-NNNN End-user documentation Troubleshooting DOC-TSH-NNNN Troubleshooting guides Reference DOC-REF-NNNN Glossary, env vars, standards Service DOC-SVC-NNNN External service integrations Tool DOC-TOOL-NNNN Agent tool documentation FAQ DOC-FAQ-NNNN Frequently asked questions Security DOC-SEC-NNNN Security documentation

docs.title: Using the Documentation Standards Agent
docs.text: The Doc Standards Agent (/standardize-doc) automatically applies this template to any document.

docs.title: From the Hive Chat (Admin Only)
docs.text:
/standardize-doc                          # Full DocSite alignment scan + auto-fix
/standardize-doc --dry-run                # Scan only — compliance report, no changes
/standardize-doc docs/my-document.md      # Standardize a single file
/standardize-doc docs/my-document.md --full-rewrite
/standardize-doc docs/my-document.md --urls https://github.com/project/README.md

docs.title: From VS Code / CLI
docs.text:
# Full DocSite alignment (scan + auto-fix all non-compliant docs)
python -m agents.specialized.doc_standards_agent

# Full scan — report only, no changes
python -m agents.specialized.doc_standards_agent --dry-run

# Single file
python -m agents.specialized.doc_standards_agent docs/my-doc.md
python -m agents.specialized.doc_standards_agent docs/my-doc.md --full-rewrite
python -m agents.specialized.doc_standards_agent docs/my-doc.md --dry-run
python -m agents.specialized.doc_standards_agent docs/my-doc.md --urls https://example.com/docs

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Flag Description --model Override LLM model for content generation --source-ref Set the source reference URL or project name --urls External URLs to fetch for citation enrichment --full-rewrite Complete LLM rewrite instead of incremental enhancement --dry-run Analyze only — show compliance report without modifying

docs.title: What the Agent Does
docs.text:
Single-file mode (/standardize-doc <filepath>):

  1. Analyzes the document structure against this standard
  2. Assigns a persistent Document ID (DOC-CATEGORY-NNNN)
  3. Enforces YAML frontmatter with all required fields
  4. Generates missing sections using the LLM
  5. Fetches external URLs for citation context (if provided)
  6. Applies glightbox attributes to all images for zoom/expand
  7. Reports before/after compliance metrics

Batch mode (/standardize-doc with no file):

  1. Discovers all .md files in docs-site/docs/ and docs/
  2. Scans every file and computes compliance percentages
  3. Reports a full compliance table sorted by worst-first
  4. Auto-fixes every file below 80% compliance (incremental enhancement)
  5. Summarizes total fixed, failed, and remaining

docs.title: Images & Screenshots
docs.text:
All images automatically support click-to-zoom via the glightbox plugin. Use the { loading=lazy } attribute:

![Network topology](../assets/topology.png){ loading=lazy }

docs.title: Mermaid Diagrams
docs.text:
Mermaid diagrams render inline and support zoom via glightbox:

```mermaid
graph TB
    A[User] --> B[Router]
    B --> C[Agent]
```

docs.title: Tables
docs.text:
Large tables automatically get horizontal scroll. Wrap in a div for extra control:

<div class="table-wrapper" markdown>
| Col 1 | Col 2 | Col 3 | Col 4 | Col 5 |
|-------|-------|-------|-------|-------|
| data  | data  | data  | data  | data  |
</div>

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• Reference: Changelog
  • Reference: Contributing
  • Reference: Environment Variables

docs.title: Environment Variables Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all environment variables used in network.env.

docs.title: Model Configuration
docs.text: Variable Description Default SOLVER_MODEL Primary response generation model qwen3.6:27b ROUTER_MODEL Intent classification model nemotron-orchestrator:8b VERIFIER_MODEL Safety/quality verification model llama-guard-3:8b

docs.title: Ollama
docs.text: Variable Description Default OLLAMA_HOST Ollama server address 192.168.2.101 OLLAMA_PORT Ollama API port 11434 OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION Enable Flash Attention 1 OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL Max concurrent requests 4 OLLAMA_MAX_LOADED_MODELS Max models in VRAM 3 OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE Idle model unload timeout 10m

docs.title: Node Addresses
docs.text: Variable Description Default HOPPER_IP Control Plane address 192.168.2.102 LOVELACE_IP Execution Plane address 192.168.2.101 TURING_IP Gateway / Turing address 192.168.2.103

docs.title: Langfuse
docs.text: Variable Description LANGFUSE_HOST Langfuse server URL LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY Langfuse public API key LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY Langfuse secret key

docs.title: PostgreSQL
docs.text: Variable Description POSTGRES_HOST PostgreSQL server address POSTGRES_PORT PostgreSQL port (default: 5432) POSTGRES_USER Database username POSTGRES_PASSWORD Database password POSTGRES_DB Database name

docs.title: Home Assistant
docs.text: Variable Description HA_URL Home Assistant API URL HA_TOKEN Long-lived access token

docs.title: SPIRE
docs.text: Variable Description SPIRE_SERVER_ADDRESS SPIRE server endpoint SPIRE_TRUST_DOMAIN Trust domain (e.g., home-ai-lab)

docs.title: ComfyUI
docs.text: Variable Description Default COMFYUI_HOST ComfyUI server address 192.168.2.101 COMFYUI_PORT ComfyUI API port 8188

docs.title: Agent Runtime
docs.text:
Variable Description Default AGENT_RUNTIME_PORT API listen port 8008 STREAM_TIMEOUT SSE stream timeout (seconds) 120 LOG_LEVEL Logging level INFO

Security

Never commit network.env to version control. It contains secrets. See Secrets Management.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
| Term | Definition |

|------|-----------|

| Action Figure Pipeline | Specialized 3D generation workflow optimized for character models |

| Agent | A specialized component that handles a specific category of tasks (e.g., Image Agent, IoT Agent) |

| Agent Runtime | The main Python application that hosts all agents, the router, coordinator, and tools |

| ComfyUI | Node-based image/video generation tool used for AI art and 3D reference images |

| Control Node | The server (192.168.2.102) running SPIRE, PostgreSQL, and Langfuse |

| Coordinator | The orchestration component that manages agent dispatch and multi-step workflows |

| Dispatcher | Task queue manager that routes jobs to appropriate agents |

| Execution Node | The server (192.168.2.101) running Ollama, Agent Runtime, ComfyUI, and voice services |

| Gateway Node | The Turing server (192.168.2.103) running Traefik, jacquard, hollerith, and knuth |

| GRPO | Group Relative Policy Optimization  the preference training algorithm used by MarsRL |

| Hive UI | The web-based chat interface for interacting with Memex |

| Intent | The classified purpose of a user message (e.g., general_chat, image_generation, iot_control) |

| Langfuse | Open-source LLM observability platform used for tracing and analytics |

| knuth | Log aggregation system by hollerith Labs |

| MarsRL | Mars Reinforcement Learning  the solve ? verify ? reward loop that ensures response quality |

| MemPalace | The vector memory system using PostgreSQL + pgvector for semantic retrieval |

| mTLS | Mutual TLS  both client and server authenticate each other with certificates |

| Ollama | Local LLM inference server that runs models on GPU |

| pgvector | PostgreSQL extension for vector similarity search, used for embeddings |

| Piper | Local text-to-speech (TTS) engine |

| jacquard | Metrics collection and alerting system |

| Router | The intent classification component that determines which agent handles a request |

| Skills Memory | Long-term memory system that stores user preferences and learned patterns |

| Solver | The primary LLM model that generates responses to user queries |

| SPIFFE | Secure Production Identity Framework for Everyone  standard for service identity |

| SPIRE | SPIFFE Runtime Environment  provides cryptographic identity (SVIDs) to services |

| SVID | SPIFFE Verifiable Identity Document  a short-lived X.509 certificate |

| Traefik | Cloud-native reverse proxy and load balancer |

| Verifier | The LLM model that checks response quality and safety before delivery |

| VRAM | Video RAM  GPU memory used for model inference |

| Whisper | OpenAI's speech-to-text (STT) model |

| Workflow | A ComfyUI node graph defining an image/video generation pipeline |
